_info: Wonniland Pokemon database. All 1500+ Pokemon and forms.

pokemon.name: Bulbasaur
pokemon.description: Bulbasaur can be seen napping in bright sunlight. There is a seed on its back. By soaking up the sun's rays, the seed grows progressively larger.

pokemon.name: Ivysaur
pokemon.description: To support its bulb, Ivysaur's legs grow sturdy. If it spends more time lying in the sunlight, the bud will soon bloom into a large flower.

pokemon.name: Venusaur
pokemon.description: Venusaur's flower is said to take on vivid colors if it gets plenty of nutrition and sunlight. The flower's aroma soothes the emotions of people.

pokemon.name: Mega-Venusaur
pokemon.description: In order to support its flower, which has grown larger due to Mega Evolution, its back and legs have become stronger.

pokemon.name: GMax-Venusaur
pokemon.description: Spits big amounts of pollen like a volcano. Breathing too much of it causes fainting. In battle, it swings around two thick vines that can easily topple 10-story buildings.

pokemon.name: Charmander
pokemon.description: The flame that burns at the tip of its tail is an indication of its emotions. The flame wavers when Charmander is happy, and blazes when it is enraged.

pokemon.name: Charmeleon
pokemon.description: Without pity, its sharp claws destroy foes. If it encounters a strong enemy, it becomes agitated, and the flame on its tail flares with a bluish white color.

pokemon.name: Charizard
pokemon.description: A Charizard flies about in search of strong opponents. It breathes intense flames that can melt any material. However, it will never torch a weaker foe.

pokemon.name: Mega-Charizard-X
pokemon.description: The overwhelming power that fills its entire body causes it to turn black and creates intense blue flames.

pokemon.name: Mega-Charizard-Y
pokemon.description: Its bond with its Trainer is the source of its power. It boasts speed and maneuverability greater than that of a jet fighter.

pokemon.name: GMax-Charizard
pokemon.description: The flame inside its body burns hotter than 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit. When Charizard roars, that temperature climbs even higher.

pokemon.name: Squirtle
pokemon.description: Its shell is not just for protection. Its rounded shape and the grooves on its surface minimize resistance in water, enabling Squirtle to swim at high speeds.

pokemon.name: Wartortle
pokemon.description: Its large tail is covered with rich, thick fur that deepens in color with age. The scratches on its shell are evidence of this Pokémon's toughness in battle.

pokemon.name: Blastoise
pokemon.description: The waterspouts that protrude from its shell are highly accurate. Their bullets of water can precisely nail tin cans from a distance of over 160 feet.

pokemon.name: Mega-Blastoise
pokemon.description: The cannon on its back is as powerful as a tank gun. Its tough legs and back enable it to withstand the recoil from firing the cannon.

pokemon.name: GMax-Blastoise
pokemon.description: It's not very good at precision shooting. When attacking, it just fires its 31 cannons over and over and over.

pokemon.name: Caterpie
pokemon.description: Its voracious appetite compels it to devour leaves bigger than itself without hesitation. It releases a terribly strong odor from its antennae.

pokemon.name: Metapod
pokemon.description: Its shell is as hard as an iron slab. A Metapod does not move very much because it is preparing its soft innards for evolution inside the shell.

pokemon.name: Butterfree
pokemon.description: It has a superior ability to search for delicious honey from flowers. It can seek, extract, and carry honey from flowers blooming over six miles away.

pokemon.name: GMax-Butterfree
pokemon.description: Once it has opponents trapped in a tornado that could blow away a 10- ton truck, it finishes them off with its poisonous scales.

pokemon.name: Weedle
pokemon.description: A Weedle has an extremely acute sense of smell. It distinguishes its favorite kinds of leaves from those it dislikes by sniffing with its big red nose.

pokemon.name: Kakuna
pokemon.description: It remains virtually immobile while it clings to a tree. However, on the inside, it busily prepares for evolution. This is evident from how hot its shell becomes.

pokemon.name: Beedrill
pokemon.description: A Beedrill is extremely territorial. For safety reasons, no one should ever approach its nest. If angered, they will attack in a swarm.

pokemon.name: Mega-Beedrill
pokemon.description: Its legs have become poison stingers. It stabs its prey repeatedly with the stingers on its limbs, dealing the final blow with the stinger on its rear.

pokemon.name: Pidgey
pokemon.description: It has an extremely sharp sense of direction. It can unerringly return home to its nest, however far it may be removed from its familiar surroundings.

pokemon.name: Pidgeotto
pokemon.description: This Pokémon flies around, patrolling its large territory. If its living space is violated, it shows no mercy in thoroughly punishing the foe with its sharp claws.

pokemon.name: Pidgeot
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has gorgeous, glossy feathers. Many Trainers are so captivated by the beautiful feathers on its head that they choose Pidgeot as their Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Mega-Pidgeot
pokemon.description: With its muscular strength now greatly increased, it can fly continuously for two weeks without resting.

pokemon.name: Rattata
pokemon.description: A Rattata is cautious in the extreme. Even while it is asleep, it constantly moves its ears and listens for danger. It will make its nest anywhere.

pokemon.name: Raticate
pokemon.description: A Raticate's sturdy fangs grow steadily. To keep them ground down, it gnaws on rocks and logs. It may even chew on the walls of houses.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Rattata
pokemon.description: With its incisors, it gnaws through doors and infiltrates people's homes. Then, with a twitch of its whiskers, it steals whatever food it finds.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Raticate
pokemon.description: It forms a group of Rattata, which it assumes command of. Each group has its own territory, and disputes over food happen often.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Raticate-Totem
pokemon.description: It forms a group of Rattata, which it assumes command of. Each group has its own territory, and disputes over food happen often.

pokemon.name: Spearow
pokemon.description: Its loud cry can be heard over half a mile away. If its high, keening cry is heard echoing all around, it is a sign that they are warning each other of danger.

pokemon.name: Fearow
pokemon.description: Its long neck and elongated beak are ideal for catching prey in soil or water. It deftly moves this extended and skinny beak to pluck prey.

pokemon.name: Ekans
pokemon.description: An Ekans curls itself up in a spiral while it rests. This position allows it to quickly respond to an enemy from any direction with a threat from its upraised head.

pokemon.name: Arbok
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has a terrifically strong constricting power. It can even flatten steel oil drums. Once it wraps its body around its foe, escaping is impossible.

pokemon.name: Pichu
pokemon.description: It is still inept at retaining electricity. When it is startled, it discharges power accidentally. It gets better at holding power as it grows older.

pokemon.name: Spiky-Ear-Pichu
pokemon.description: It is still inept at retaining electricity. When it is startled, it discharges power accidentally. It gets better at holding power as it grows older.

pokemon.name: Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Cosplay-Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Rock-Star-Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Belle-Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Pop-Star-Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: PHD-Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Libre-Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Original-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed during adventures across many different regions.

pokemon.name: Hoenn-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed during adventures across the Hoenn region together.

pokemon.name: Sinnoh-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed during adventures across the Sinnoh region together.

pokemon.name: Unova-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed during adventures across the Unova region together.

pokemon.name: Kalos-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed during adventures across the Kalos region together.

pokemon.name: Alola-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed during adventures across the Alola region together.

pokemon.name: Partner-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed as the two overcame many hardships together.

pokemon.name: World-Pikachu
pokemon.description: This Pikachu wears its partner's cap, which is proof of the strong bond Pikachu and its partner formed during adventures across the world together.

pokemon.name: GMax-Pikachu
pokemon.description: When it smashes its opponents with its bolt-shaped tail, it delivers a surge of electricity equivalent to a lightning strike.

pokemon.name: Starter-Pikachu
pokemon.description: It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Raichu
pokemon.description: If it stores too much electricity, its behavior turns aggressive. To avoid this, it occasionally discharges excess energy and calms itself down.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Raichu
pokemon.description: It uses psychokinesis to control electricity. It focuses psychic energy into its tail and rides it like it's surfing. Another name for this Pokémon is 'hodad'.

pokemon.name: Mega-Raichu-X
pokemon.description: It resembles an X as it flies through the air with 50 million volts of electricity sparking from its ears and forked tail.

pokemon.name: Mega-Raichu-Y
pokemon.description: It fires bolts of electricity from the tip of its tail and from the spiky tufts of fur growing out of its temples. This electricity forms the letter Y.

pokemon.name: Sandshrew
pokemon.description: When it curls up in a ball, it can make any attack bounce off harmlessly. Its hide has turned tough and solid as a result of living in the desert.

pokemon.name: Sandslash
pokemon.description: It curls up in a ball to protect itself from enemy attacks. It also curls up to prevent heatstroke during the daytime when temperatures rise sharply.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Sandshrew
pokemon.description: Life on mountains covered with deep snow has granted this Pokémon a body of ice that's as hard as steel. Predators go after its soft belly.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Sandslash
pokemon.description: It uses large, hooked claws to cut a path through deep snow as it runs. On snowy mountains, this Sandslash is faster than any other Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Nidoran♀
pokemon.description: Its highly toxic barbs are thought to have developed as protection for this small- bodied Pokémon. When enraged, it releases a horrible toxin from its horn.

pokemon.name: Nidorina
pokemon.description: When it is with its friends or family, its barbs are tucked away to prevent injury. It appears to become nervous if separated from the others.

pokemon.name: Nidoqueen
pokemon.description: It is adept at sending foes flying with harsh tackles using its tough, scaly body. This Pokémon is at its strongest when it is defending its young.

pokemon.name: Nidoran♂
pokemon.description: The male Nidoran has developed muscles that freely move its ears in any direction. Even the slightest sound does not escape this Pokémon's notice.

pokemon.name: Nidorino
pokemon.description: Its horn is harder than a diamond. If it senses a hostile presence, all the barbs on its back bristle up at once, and it challenges the foe with all its might.

pokemon.name: Nidoking
pokemon.description: A Nidoking's thick tail packs enormously destructive power capable of toppling a metal transmission tower. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

pokemon.name: Cleffa
pokemon.description: On nights with many shooting stars, Cleffa can be seen dancing in a ring. They dance until daybreak, when they quench their thirst with the morning dew.

pokemon.name: Clefairy
pokemon.description: On every night of a full moon, they come out to play. When dawn arrives, the tired Clefairy go to sleep nestled up against each other in deep and quiet mountains.

pokemon.name: Clefable
pokemon.description: A Clefable uses its wings to skip lightly as if it were flying. Its bouncy step lets it even walk on water. On quiet, moonlit nights, it strolls on lakes.

pokemon.name: Mega-Clefable
pokemon.description: It flies by using the power of moonlight to control gravity within a radius of over 32 feet around it.

pokemon.name: Vulpix
pokemon.description: It can freely control fire, making fiery orbs fly like will-o'-the-wisps. Just before evolution, its six tails grow hot as if on fire.

pokemon.name: Ninetales
pokemon.description: It has long been said that each of the nine tails embody an enchanted power. A long-lived Ninetales will have fur that shines like gold.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Vulpix
pokemon.description: They live together in a skulk, helping one another. In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Ninetales
pokemon.description: While it will guide travelers who get lost on a snowy mountain down to the mountain's base, it won't forgive anyone who harms nature.

pokemon.name: Igglybuff
pokemon.description: Its soft and pliable body is very bouncy. When it sings continuously with all its might, its body steadily turns a deepening pink color.

pokemon.name: Jigglypuff
pokemon.description: Nothing can avoid falling asleep hearing a Jigglypuff's song. The sound waves of its singing voice match the brain waves of someone in a deep sleep.

pokemon.name: Wigglytuff
pokemon.description: Its fur is the ultimate in luxuriousness. Sleeping alongside a Wigglytuff is simply divine. Its body expands seemingly without end when it inhales.

pokemon.name: Zubat
pokemon.description: While living in pitch-black caverns, their eyes gradually grew shut and deprived them of vision. They use ultrasonic waves to detect obstacles.

pokemon.name: Golbat
pokemon.description: Its fangs easily puncture even thick animal hide. It loves to feast on the blood of people and Pokémon. It flits about in darkness and strikes from behind.

pokemon.name: Crobat
pokemon.description: Over the course of evolution, its hind legs turned into wings. By alternately resting its front and rear wings, it can fly all day without having to stop.

pokemon.name: Oddish
pokemon.description: This Pokémon grows by absorbing moonlight. During the daytime, it buries itself in the ground, leaving only its leaves exposed to avoid detection by its enemies.

pokemon.name: Gloom
pokemon.description: A horribly noxious honey drools from its mouth. One whiff of the honey can result in memory loss. Some fans are said to enjoy this overwhelming stink, however.

pokemon.name: Vileplume
pokemon.description: In seasons when it produces more pollen, the air around a Vileplume turns yellow with the powder as it walks. The pollen is highly toxic and causes paralysis.

pokemon.name: Bellossom
pokemon.description: Its flower petals deepen in color through exposure to sunlight. When cloudy weather persists, it does a dance that is thought to be a ritual for summoning the sun.

pokemon.name: Paras
pokemon.description: A Paras has parasitic tochukaso mushrooms growing on its back. They grow by drawing nutrients from the host. They are valued as a medicine for long life.

pokemon.name: Parasect
pokemon.description: Parasect are known to infest the roots of large trees en masse and drain nutrients. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.

pokemon.name: Venonat
pokemon.description: Its coat of thin, stiff hair that covers its entire body is said to have evolved for protection. Its large eyes never fail to spot even miniscule prey.

pokemon.name: Venomoth
pokemon.description: Venomoth are nocturnal--they are only active at night. Their favorite prey are insects that gather around streetlights, attracted by the light in the darkness.

pokemon.name: Diglett
pokemon.description: Diglett are raised in most farms. The reason is simple--wherever they burrow, the soil is left perfectly tilled for growing delicious crops.

pokemon.name: Dugtrio
pokemon.description: Because the triplets originally split from one body, they think exactly alike. They work cooperatively to burrow endlessly through the ground.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Diglett
pokemon.description: The metal-rich geology of its habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head that change shape depending on its mood and when communicating with others.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Dugtrio
pokemon.description: Their beautiful, metallic whiskers create a sort of protective helmet on heir heads, and they also function as highly precise sensors.

pokemon.name: Meowth
pokemon.description: Meowth withdraw their sharp claws into their paws to silently sneak about. For some reason, this Pokémon loves shiny coins that glitter with light.

pokemon.name: Persian
pokemon.description: A Persian's six bold whiskers sense air movements to determine what is in its vicinity. It becomes docile if grabbed by the whiskers.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Meowth
pokemon.description: It's accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. Highly smart and proud, it's famously difficult to handle, but that also makes it popular.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Persian
pokemon.description: It looks down on everyone other than itself. In contrast to its lovely face, it is so brutal that it tortures its weakened prey rather than finishing them off.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Meowth
pokemon.description: Living with a savage, seafaring people has hardened its body so much that parts of it turned to iron. Darker coins are harder and garner more respect among Meowth.

pokemon.name: Perrserker
pokemon.description: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

pokemon.name: GMax-Meowth
pokemon.description: The pattern that has appeared on its giant coin is thought to be the key to unlocking the secrets of the Dynamax phenomenon.

pokemon.name: Psyduck
pokemon.description: When its headache intensifies, it starts using strange powers. However, it has no recollection of its powers, so it always looks befuddled and bewildered.

pokemon.name: Golduck
pokemon.description: A Golduck is an adept swimmer. It sometimes joins competitive swimmers in training. It uses psychic powers when its forehead shimmers with light.

pokemon.name: Mankey
pokemon.description: When it starts shaking and its nasal breathing turns rough, it's a sure sign of anger. However, since this happens instantly, there is no time to flee.

pokemon.name: Primeape
pokemon.description: When it becomes furious, its blood circulation becomes more robust, and its muscles are made stronger. But it also becomes much less intelligent.

pokemon.name: Annihilape
pokemon.description: When its anger rose beyond a critical point, this Pokémon gained power that is unfettered by the limits of its physical body.

pokemon.name: Growlithe
pokemon.description: Its superb sense of smell ensures that this Pokémon won't forget any scent, no matter what. It uses its sense of smell to detect the emotions of others.

pokemon.name: Arcanine
pokemon.description: This fleet-footed Pokémon is said to run over 6,200 miles in a single day and night. The fire that blazes wildly within its body is its source of power.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Growlithe
pokemon.description: They patrol their territory in pairs. It's believed the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Arcanine
pokemon.description: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase.

pokemon.name: Poliwag
pokemon.description: It is possible to see this Pokémon's spiral innards right through its thin skin. However, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.

pokemon.name: Poliwhirl
pokemon.description: Its body surface is always wet and slick with an oily fluid. Because of this greasy covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.

pokemon.name: Poliwrath
pokemon.description: Its highly developed muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. This Pokémon can swim back and forth across the Pacific Ocean without effort.

pokemon.name: Politoed
pokemon.description: The curled hair on its head proves its status as a king. It is said that the longer and curlier the hair, the more respect it earns from its peers.

pokemon.name: Abra
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that sleeps 18 hours a day. Observation revealed that it uses Teleport to change its location once every hour.

pokemon.name: Kadabra
pokemon.description: It is rumored that a boy with psychic abilities suddenly transformed into Kadabra while he was assisting research into extrasensory powers.

pokemon.name: Alakazam
pokemon.description: While it has strong psychic abilities and high intelligence, an Alakazam's muscles are very weak. It uses psychic power to move its body.

pokemon.name: Mega-Alakazam
pokemon.description: Having traded away its muscles, Alakazam's true power has been unleashed. With its psychic powers, it can foresee all things.

pokemon.name: Machop
pokemon.description: It continually undertakes strenuous training to master all forms of martial arts. Its strength lets it easily hoist a sumo wrestler onto its shoulders.

pokemon.name: Machoke
pokemon.description: A belt is worn by a Machoke to keep its overwhelming power under control. Because it is so dangerous, no one has ever removed the belt.

pokemon.name: Machamp
pokemon.description: It is impossible to defend against punches and chops doled out by its four arms. Its fighting spirit flares up when it faces a tough opponent.

pokemon.name: GMax-Machamp
pokemon.description: One of these Pokémon once used its immeasurable strength to lift a large ship that was in trouble. It then carried the ship to port.

pokemon.name: Bellsprout
pokemon.description: A Bellsprout's thin and flexible body lets it bend and sway to avoid any attack, however strong it may be. From its mouth, it leaks a fluid that melts even iron.

pokemon.name: Weepinbell
pokemon.description: At night, a Weepinbell hangs on to a tree branch with its hooked rear and sleeps. If it moves around in its sleep, it may wake up to find itself on the ground.

pokemon.name: Victreebel
pokemon.description: The long vine extending from its head is waved about as if it were a living thing to attract prey. When an unsuspecting victim approaches, it is swallowed whole.

pokemon.name: Mega-Victreebel
pokemon.description: The volume of this Pokémon's acid has increased due to Mega Evolution, filling its mouth. If not careful, the acid will overflow and spill out.

pokemon.name: Tentacool
pokemon.description: Its body is almost entirely composed of water. It ensnares its foe with its two long tentacles, then stabs with the poison stingers at their tips.

pokemon.name: Tentacruel
pokemon.description: It lives in complex rock formations on the ocean floor and traps prey using its 80 tentacles. Its red orbs glow when it grows excited or agitated.

pokemon.name: Geodude
pokemon.description: It climbs mountain paths using only the power of its arms. Because they look just like boulders lining paths, hikers may step on them without noticing.

pokemon.name: Graveler
pokemon.description: They descend from mountains by tumbling down steep slopes. They are so brutal, they smash aside obstructing trees and massive boulders with thunderous tackles.

pokemon.name: Golem
pokemon.description: It is said to live in volcanic craters on mountain peaks. Once a year, it sheds its hide and grows larger. The shed hide crumbles and returns to the soil.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Geodude
pokemon.description: Its body is a magnetic stone. Iron sand attach to parts of its body with stronger magnetism. If you carelessly step on one, it will headbutt and shock you in anger.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Graveler
pokemon.description: When two Graveler smash each other over dravite, their favorite food, they cause flashes of light and booming noises. People call them “fireworks of the earth.”

pokemon.name: Alolan-Golem
pokemon.description: It fires rocks charged with electricity. Even if the rock isn't fired that accurately, just grazing an opponent will cause numbness and fainting.

pokemon.name: Ponyta
pokemon.description: A Ponyta is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. Its legs become stronger as it stumbles and falls while trying to keep up with its parent.

pokemon.name: Rapidash
pokemon.description: It usually canters casually in the fields and plains. But once a Rapidash turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Ponyta
pokemon.description: Its small horn hides a healing power. This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Rapidash
pokemon.description: Little can stand up to its psycho cut. Unleashed from this Pokémon's horn, the move will punch a hole right through a thick metal sheet.

pokemon.name: Slowpoke
pokemon.description: It catches prey by dipping its tail in water at the side of a river. But it often forgets what it is doing and spends entire days just loafing at water's edge.

pokemon.name: Slowbro
pokemon.description: Its tail has a Shellder firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can't be used for fishing anymore. This forces it to reluctantly swim and catch prey.

pokemon.name: Slowking
pokemon.description: It undertakes research every day to solve the mysteries of the world. However, it apparently forgets everything if the Shellder on its head comes off.

pokemon.name: Mega-Slowbro
pokemon.description: When bathed in the energy of Mega Evolution, Shellder converts into impregnable armor. There is virtually no change in Slowpoke.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Slowpoke
pokemon.description: Although it is normally zoned out, its expression abruptly sharpens on occasion. The cause seems to lie in Slowpoke's diet, which also give their tails a spicy flavor.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Slowbro
pokemon.description: A Shellder bite set off a chemical reaction with the spices inside Slowbro's body, causing Slowbro to become a Poison-type Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Slowking
pokemon.description: A combination of toxins and the shock of evolving has increased Shellder's intelligence to the point that Shellder now controls Slowking.

pokemon.name: Magnemite
pokemon.description: The units at its sides are extremely powerful magnets. They generate enough magnetism to draw in iron objects from over 300 feet away.

pokemon.name: Magneton
pokemon.description: It is actually three Magnemite linked by magnetism. It generates powerful radio waves that raise temperatures by 3.6 degrees F within a 3,300-foot radius.

pokemon.name: Magnezone
pokemon.description: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting its own signals of unknown purpose.

pokemon.name: Farfetch'd
pokemon.description: It is always seen with a stick from a plant. Apparently, there are good sticks and bad sticks. This Pokémon occasionally fights with others over choice sticks.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Farfetch'd
pokemon.description: The stalks of leeks are thicker and longer in the Galar region. Brave Farfetch'd warriors that adapted to these stalks took on a unique form.

pokemon.name: Sirfetch'd
pokemon.description: Only Farfetch'd that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon's leek withers, it will retire from combat.

pokemon.name: Doduo
pokemon.description: Even while eating or sleeping, one of the heads remains always vigilant for any sign of danger. When threatened, it flees at over 60 miles per hour.

pokemon.name: Dodrio
pokemon.description: A peculiar Pokémon species with three heads. It vigorously races across grassy plains even in arid seasons with little rainfall.

pokemon.name: Seel
pokemon.description: Seel hunt for prey in frigid, ice-covered seas. When it needs to breathe, it punches a hole through the ice with the sharply protruding section of its head.

pokemon.name: Dewgong
pokemon.description: It loves to snooze on bitterly cold ice. The sight of this Pokémon sleeping on a glacier was mistakenly thought to be a mermaid by a mariner long ago.

pokemon.name: Grimer
pokemon.description: Born from polluted sludge in the sea, Grimer's favorite food is anything filthy. They feed on wastewater pumped out from factories.

pokemon.name: Muk
pokemon.description: It prefers warm and humid habitats. In the summertime, the toxic substances in its body intensify, making Muk reek like putrid kitchen garbage.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Grimer
pokemon.description: There are a hundred or so of them living in Alola's waste-disposal site. They're all hard workers who eat a lot of trash. Grimer seems to relish any and all kinds of trash.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Muk
pokemon.description: While it's unexpectedly quiet and friendly, if it's not fed any trash for a while,, it will smash its Trainer's furnishings, and eat up the fragments.

pokemon.name: Shellder
pokemon.description: At night, it burrows a hole in the seafloor with its broad tongue to make a place to sleep. While asleep, it closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.

pokemon.name: Cloyster
pokemon.description: It swims in the sea by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. The Cloyster shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.

pokemon.name: Gastly
pokemon.description: When exposed to a strong wind, a Gastly's gaseous body quickly dwindles away. They cluster under the eaves of houses to escape the ravages of wind.

pokemon.name: Haunter
pokemon.description: If a Haunter beckons you while it is floating in darkness, don't approach it. This Pokémon will try to lick you with its tongue and steal your life away.

pokemon.name: Gengar
pokemon.description: Deep in the night, your shadow cast by a streetlight may suddenly overtake you. It is actually a Gengar running past you, pretending to be your shadow.

pokemon.name: Mega-Gengar
pokemon.description: Gengar's relationships are warped. It tries to take the lives of anyone and everyone. It will even try to curse the Trainer who is its master!

pokemon.name: GMax-Gengar
pokemon.description: Rumor has it that its gigantic mouth leads not into its body, filled with cursed energy, but instead directly to the afterlife.

pokemon.name: Onix
pokemon.description: There is a magnet in its brain that prevents an Onix from losing direction while tunneling. As it grows older, its body becomes steadily rounder and smoother.

pokemon.name: Steelix
pokemon.description: Steelix live even further underground than Onix. This Pokémon is known to dig toward the earth's core, reaching a depth of over six-tenths of a mile underground.

pokemon.name: Mega-Steelix
pokemon.description: The cells within its body, crystallized by the energy produced from Mega Evolution, are stronger than any mineral and able to withstand any temperature.

pokemon.name: Drowzee
pokemon.description: If your nose becomes itchy while you are sleeping, it's a sure sign that a Drowzee is standing above your pillow and trying to eat your dream through your nostrils.

pokemon.name: Hypno
pokemon.description: The arcing movement and glitter of the pendulum in a Hypno's hand lull the foe into deep hypnosis. While searching for prey, it polishes the pendulum.

pokemon.name: Krabby
pokemon.description: Krabby live in holes dug into beaches. On sandy shores with little in the way of food, they can be seen squabbling with each other over territory.

pokemon.name: Kingler
pokemon.description: It waves its huge, oversized claw in the air to communicate with others. But since the claw is so heavy, this Pokémon quickly tires.

pokemon.name: GMax-Kingler
pokemon.description: The flow of Gigantamax energy has spurred this Pokémon's left pincer to grow to an enormous size. That claw can pulverize anything.

pokemon.name: Voltorb
pokemon.description: It bears an uncanny and unexplained resemblance to a Poké Ball. Because it explodes at the slightest shock, even veteran Trainers treat it with caution.

pokemon.name: Electrode
pokemon.description: They appear in great numbers at electric power plants. Because they feed on electricity, they cause massive and chaotic blackouts in nearby cities.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Voltorb
pokemon.description: It resembles an ancient Poké Ball design. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Electrode
pokemon.description: The tissue on the surface of its body is similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, it lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.

pokemon.name: Exeggcute
pokemon.description: It consists of six eggs that care for each other. The eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear, it is close to evolution.

pokemon.name: Exeggutor
pokemon.description: Originally from the tropics, Exeggutor's heads grow larger from exposure to strong sunlight. It is said that when the heads fall, they group to form an Exeggcute.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Exeggutor
pokemon.description: Alola is the best environment for this Pokémon. Local people take pride in its appearance, saying this is how Exeggutor ought to look.

pokemon.name: Cubone
pokemon.description: It pines for the mother it will never see again. Seeing a likeness of its mother in the full moon, it cries. The stains on the skull it wears are from its tears.

pokemon.name: Marowak
pokemon.description: A Marowak is the evolved form of a Cubone that has grown tough by overcoming the grief of losing its mother. Its tempered and hardened spirit is not easily broken.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Marowak
pokemon.description: The cursed flames that light up the bone carried by this Pokémon are said to cause both mental and physical pain that will never fade.

pokemon.name: Alolan-Marowak (Totem)
pokemon.description: The cursed flames that light up the bone carried by this Pokémon are said to cause both mental and physical pain that will never fade.

pokemon.name: Tyrogue
pokemon.description: Tyrogue become stressed out if they do not get to train every day. When raising this Pokémon, the Trainer must establish a regular training schedule.

pokemon.name: Hitmonlee
pokemon.description: Its legs freely stretch and contract. Using these springlike limbs, it bowls over foes with devastating kicks. After battle, it rubs down its tired legs.

pokemon.name: Hitmonchan
pokemon.description: A Hitmonchan is said to possess the spirit of a boxer who aimed to become the world champion. Having an indomitable spirit means that it will never give up.

pokemon.name: Hitmontop
pokemon.description: Its technique of kicking while spinning is a remarkable mix of both offense and defense. Hitmontop travel faster spinning than they do walking.

pokemon.name: Lickitung
pokemon.description: Whenever it sees something unfamiliar, it always licks the object because it memorizes things by texture and taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.

pokemon.name: Lickilicky
pokemon.description: The long tongue is always soggy with slobber. The saliva contains a solvent that causes numbness. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.

pokemon.name: Koffing
pokemon.description: Getting up close to a Koffing will give you a chance to observe, through its thin skin, the toxic gases swirling inside. It blows up at the slightest stimulation.

pokemon.name: Weezing
pokemon.description: By diluting its toxic gases with a special process, the highest grade of perfume can be extracted. To Weezing, gases emanating from garbage are the ultimate feast.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Weezing
pokemon.description: Long ago, when droves of factories fouled the air with pollution, it changed into this form for some reason. It consumes air pollutant particles, expelling clean air.

pokemon.name: Rhyhorn
pokemon.description: Once it starts running, it doesn't stop. Its tiny brain makes it so stupid that it can't remember why it started running in the first place.

pokemon.name: Rhydon
pokemon.description: Its horn, which rotates like a drill, destroys tall buildings with one strike. It stands on its hind legs, and its brain is well developed.

pokemon.name: Rhyperior
pokemon.description: It can launch a rock held in its hand like a missile by tightening and then expanding its muscles instantaneously. Geodude are shot at rare times.

pokemon.name: Happiny
pokemon.description: It carries a round white rock in its belly pouch. If it gets along well with someone, it will sometimes give that person the rock.

pokemon.name: Chansey
pokemon.description: Chansey lay nutritionally excellent eggs every day. The eggs are so delicious, they are eagerly devoured by even those people who have lost their appetite.

pokemon.name: Blissey
pokemon.description: If it senses sadness with its fluffy fur, a Blissey will rush over to the sad person, however far away, to share an egg of happiness that brings a smile to any face.

pokemon.name: Tangela
pokemon.description: Its vines snap off easily and painlessly if they are grabbed, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by new growth the very next day.

pokemon.name: Tangrowth
pokemon.description: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Even if one of its arms is eaten, it's fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.

pokemon.name: Kangaskhan
pokemon.description: If you come across a young Kangaskhan playing by itself, never try to catch it. The baby's parent is sure to be in the area, and it will become violently enraged.

pokemon.name: Mega-Kangaskhan
pokemon.description: When the mother sees the back of her Mega-Evolved child, it makes her think of the day when her child will inevitably leave her.

pokemon.name: Horsea
pokemon.description: By cleverly flicking the fins on its back side to side, it moves in any direction while facing forward. It spits ink to escape if it senses danger.

pokemon.name: Seadra
pokemon.description: The poisonous barbs all over its body are highly valued as ingredients for making traditional herbal medicine. It shows no mercy to anything approaching its nest.

pokemon.name: Kingdra
pokemon.description: It sleeps quietly, deep on the seafloor. When it comes up to the surface, it creates a huge whirlpool that can swallow even ships.

pokemon.name: Goldeen
pokemon.description: In the springtime, schools of Goldeen can be seen swimming up falls and rivers. It metes out staggering damage with its single horn.

pokemon.name: Seaking
pokemon.description: It punches holes in boulders on stream- beds. This is a clever innovation that prevents its eggs from being attacked or washed away by the current.

pokemon.name: Staryu
pokemon.description: It gathers with others in the night and makes its red core glow on and off with the twinkling stars. It can regenerate limbs if they are severed from its body.

pokemon.name: Starmie
pokemon.description: People in ancient times imagined that Starmie were transformed from the reflections of stars that twinkled on gentle waves at night.

pokemon.name: Mega-Starmie
pokemon.description: Its movements have become more humanlike. Whether it's simply trying to communicate or wants to supplant humanity is unclear.

pokemon.name: Mime Jr.
pokemon.description: In an attempt to confuse its enemy, it mimics the enemy's movements. Once mimicked, the foe cannot take its eyes off this Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Mr. Mime
pokemon.description: A Mr. Mime is a master of pantomime. It can convince others that something unseeable actually exists. Once believed, the imaginary object does become real.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Mr. Mime
pokemon.description: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet to to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.

pokemon.name: Mr. Rime
pokemon.description: It's highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

pokemon.name: Scyther
pokemon.description: Its blindingly fast speed adds to the sharpness of its twin forearm scythes. The scythes can slice through thick logs in one wicked stroke.

pokemon.name: Scizor
pokemon.description: A Scizor has a body with the hardness of steel. It is not easily fazed by ordinary sorts of attacks. It flaps its wings to regulate its body temperature.

pokemon.name: Mega-Scizor
pokemon.description: The excess energy that bathes this Pokémon keeps it in constant danger of overflow. It can't sustain a battle over long periods of time.

pokemon.name: Kleavor
pokemon.description: A violent creature that fells trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. Should one encounter this Pokémon in the wild, one's only recourse is to flee.

pokemon.name: Smoochum
pokemon.description: It actively runs about, but also falls often. Whenever it falls, it will check its reflection on a lake's surface to make sure its face hasn't become dirty.

pokemon.name: Jynx
pokemon.description: A Jynx sashays rhythmically as if it were dancing. Its motions are so bouncingly alluring, people seeing it are compelled to shake their hips without noticing.

pokemon.name: Elekid
pokemon.description: If it touches metal and discharges the electricity it has stored in its body, an Elekid begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.

pokemon.name: Electabuzz
pokemon.description: When a storm approaches, it competes with others to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.

pokemon.name: Electivire
pokemon.description: When it gets excited, it thumps its chest. With every thud, thunder roars, electric sparks shower all around and blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.

pokemon.name: Magby
pokemon.description: If a Magby is spouting yellow flames from its mouth, it is in good health. When it is fatigued, black smoke will be mixed in with the flames.

pokemon.name: Magmar
pokemon.description: In battle, it blows out intense flames from all over its body to intimidate its foe. These fiery bursts create heat waves that ignite grass and trees in the area.

pokemon.name: Magmortar
pokemon.description: According to what is known, a single pair of male and female Magmortar lives in one volcano. From its arm, it launches fireballs hotter than 3,600ºF.

pokemon.name: Pinsir
pokemon.description: Their pincers are strong enough to shatter thick logs. Because they dislike cold, Pinsir burrow and sleep under the ground on chilly nights.

pokemon.name: Mega-Pinsir
pokemon.description: The influence of Mega Evolution leaves it in a state of constant excitement. It pierces enemies with its two large horns before shredding them.

pokemon.name: Tauros
pokemon.description: It is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for Tauros to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.

pokemon.name: Paldean-Tauros-Combat
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has a muscular body and excels at close-quarters combat. It uses its short horns to strike the opponent’s weak spots.

pokemon.name: Paldean-Tauros-Blaze
pokemon.description: When heated by fire energy, its horns can get hotter than 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. Those gored by them will suffer both wounds and burns.

pokemon.name: Paldean-Tauros-Aqua
pokemon.description: This Pokémon blasts water from holes on the tips of its horns--the high-pressure jets pierce right through Tauros’s enemies.

pokemon.name: Magikarp
pokemon.description: Its swimming muscles are weak, so it is easily washed away by currents. In places where water pools, you can see many Magikarp deposited there by the flow.

pokemon.name: Gyarados
pokemon.description: It is an extremely vicious and violent Pokémon. When humans begin to fight, it will appear and burn everything to the ground with intensely hot flames.

pokemon.name: Mega-Gyarados
pokemon.description: Although it obeys its instinctive drive to destroy everything within its reach, it will respond to orders from a Trainer it truly trusts.

pokemon.name: Lapras
pokemon.description: People have driven Lapras almost to the point of extinction. In the evenings, it is said to sing plaintively as it seeks what few others of its kind still remain.

pokemon.name: GMax-Lapras
pokemon.description: Over 5,000 people can ride on its shell at once. And it's a very comfortable ride, without the slightest shaking or swaying.

pokemon.name: Ditto
pokemon.description: A Ditto rearranges its cell structure to transform itself. However, if it tries to change based on its memory, it will get details wrong.

pokemon.name: Eevee
pokemon.description: An Eevee has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to its environment. Radiation from various Stones causes this Pokémon to evolve.

pokemon.name: GMax-Eevee
pokemon.description: Having gotten even friendlier and more innocent, Eevee tries to play with anyone around, only to end up crushing them with its immense body.

pokemon.name: Starter-Eevee
pokemon.description: An Eevee has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to its environment. Radiation from various Stones causes this Pokémon to evolve.

pokemon.name: Vaporeon
pokemon.description: Vaporeon underwent a spontaneous mutation and grew fins and gills that allow them to live underwater. They have the ability to freely control water.

pokemon.name: Jolteon
pokemon.description: Its cells generate weak power that is amplified by its fur's static electricity to drop thunderbolts. The bristling fur is made of electrically charged needles.

pokemon.name: Flareon
pokemon.description: Flareon's fluffy fur releases heat into the air so that its body does not get excessively hot. Its body temperature can rise to a maximum of 1,650 degrees F.

pokemon.name: Espeon
pokemon.description: An Espeon is extremely loyal to any Trainer it considers to be worthy. It is said to have developed precognitive powers to protect its Trainer from harm.

pokemon.name: Umbreon
pokemon.description: Umbreon evolved from exposure to the moon's energy pulses. It lurks in darkness and waits for its foes to move. The rings on its body glow when it leaps to attack.

pokemon.name: Leafeon
pokemon.description: Its cellular composition is closer to that of a plant than an animal. It uses photosynthesis to produce its energy supply without eating food.

pokemon.name: Glaceon
pokemon.description: It can control its body temperature at will. This enables it to freeze the moisture in the atmosphere, creating flurries of diamond dust.

pokemon.name: Sylveon
pokemon.description: Its ribbonlike feelers give off an aura that weakens hostility in its prey, causing them to let down their guard. A moment later, it pounces.

pokemon.name: Porygon
pokemon.description: It is capable of reverting itself entirely back to program data in order to enter cyberspace. A Porygon is copy- protected so it cannot be duplicated.

pokemon.name: Porygon2
pokemon.description: It was created by humans using the power of science. It has been given artificial intelligence that enables it to learn new gestures and emotions on its own.

pokemon.name: Porygon-Z
pokemon.description: In order to create a more advanced Pokémon, an additional program was installed, but apparently it contained a defect that made it move oddly.

pokemon.name: Omanyte
pokemon.description: One of the ancient and long-since-extinct Pokémon that have been regenerated from fossils by humans. If attacked, it withdraws into its hard shell.

pokemon.name: Omastar
pokemon.description: An Omastar uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large, making its movements slow and ponderous.

pokemon.name: Kabuto
pokemon.description: It is a Pokémon that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in rare cases, living examples have been discovered. Kabuto have not changed for 300 million years.

pokemon.name: Kabutops
pokemon.description: Kabutops once swam underwater to hunt for prey. It was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from changes in its gills and legs.

pokemon.name: Aerodactyl
pokemon.description: Aerodactyl is a Pokémon from the age of dinosaurs. It was regenerated from DNA extracted from amber. It is imagined to have been the king of the skies.

pokemon.name: Mega-Aerodactyl
pokemon.description: The power of Mega Evolution has completely restored its genes. The rocks on its body are harder than diamond.

pokemon.name: Munchlax
pokemon.description: When it finds something that looks like it might be edible, it goes right ahead and swallows it whole. That's why it gets fatter day by day.

pokemon.name: Snorlax
pokemon.description: Snorlax's typical day consists of nothing more than eating and sleeping. It is such a docile Pokémon that there are children who use its big belly as a place to play.

pokemon.name: GMax-Snorlax
pokemon.description: Gigantamax energy has affected stray seeds and even pebbles that got stuck to Snorlax, making them grow to a huge size.

pokemon.name: Articuno
pokemon.description: Articuno is a legendary bird Pokémon that can control ice. The flapping of its wings chills the air. As a result, it is said that when this Pokémon flies, snow will fall.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Articuno
pokemon.description: It fires beams that immobilize opponents as if they had been frozen solid. Its feather-like blades of psychic energy rip through thick iron sheets like paper.

pokemon.name: Zapdos
pokemon.description: Zapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon that has the ability to control electricity. It usually lives in thunderclouds. It gains power if it is stricken by lightning bolts.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Zapdos
pokemon.description: When its feathers rub together, they produce a crackling sound like the zapping of electricity. Apparently, it runs through the mountains at over 180 mph.

pokemon.name: Moltres
pokemon.description: Moltres is a legendary bird Pokémon that can control fire. If injured, it is said to dip its body in the molten magma of a volcano to burn and heal itself.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Moltres
pokemon.description: This Pokémon's sinister, flame-like aura will consume the spirit of any creature it hits. Victims become burned-out shadows of themselves.

pokemon.name: Dratini
pokemon.description: A Dratini continually molts and sloughs off its old skin. It does so because the life energy within its body steadily builds to reach uncontrollable levels.

pokemon.name: Dragonair
pokemon.description: A Dragonair stores an enormous amount of energy inside its body. It is said to alter the weather around it by loosing energy from the crystals on its neck and tail.

pokemon.name: Dragonite
pokemon.description: It can circle the globe in just 16 hours. It is a kindhearted Pokémon that leads lost and foundering ships in a storm to the safety of land.

pokemon.name: Mega-Dragonite
pokemon.description: Mega Evolution has excessively powered up this Pokémon's feelings of kindness. It finishes off its opponents with mercy in its heart.

pokemon.name: Mewtwo
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that was created by genetic manipulation. However, even though the scientific power of humans made its body, they failed to give it a warm heart.

pokemon.name: Mega-Mewtwo-X
pokemon.description: Psychic power has augmented its muscles. It has a grip strength of one ton and can sprint a hundred meters in two seconds flat!

pokemon.name: Mega-Mewtwo-Y
pokemon.description: Despite its diminished size, its mental power has grown phenomenally. With a mere thought, it can smash a skyscraper to smithereens.

pokemon.name: Mew
pokemon.description: A Mew is said to possess the genes of all Pokémon. It is capable of making itself invisible at will, so it entirely avoids notice even if it approaches people.

pokemon.name: Chikorita
pokemon.description: It waves its leaf around to keep foes at bay. However, a sweet fragrance also wafts from the leaf, creating a friendly atmosphere that becalms the battlers.

pokemon.name: Bayleef
pokemon.description: A Bayleef's neck is ringed by curled-up leaves. Inside each leaf is a small tree shoot. The fragrance of this shoot makes people peppy.

pokemon.name: Meganium
pokemon.description: The fragrance of a Meganium's flower soothes and calms emotions. In battle, it gives off more of its becalming scent to blunt the foe's fighting spirit.

pokemon.name: Mega-Meganium
pokemon.description: This Pokémon can fire a tremendously powerful Solar Beam from its four flowers. Another name for this is Mega Sol Cannon.

pokemon.name: Cyndaquil
pokemon.description: It flares flames from its back to protect itself. The fire burns vigorously if the Pokémon is angry. When it is tired, it sputters with incomplete combustion.

pokemon.name: Quilava
pokemon.description: It intimidates foes with intense gusts of flames and superheated air. Its quick nimbleness lets it dodge attacks even while scorching an enemy.

pokemon.name: Typhlosion
pokemon.description: It can hide behind a shimmering heat haze that it creates using its intense flames. Typhlosion create blazing explosive blasts that burn everything to cinders.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Typhlosion
pokemon.description: Said to purify lost, forsaken souls with its flames and guide them to the afterlife. It's believed its form was influenced by the energy of Mt. Coronet in ancient times.

pokemon.name: Totodile
pokemon.description: Despite its small body, Totodile's jaws are very powerful. While it may think it is just playfully nipping, its bite has enough strength to cause serious injury.

pokemon.name: Croconaw
pokemon.description: Once its jaws clamp down on its foe, it will absolutely not let go. Because the tips of its fangs are forked back like fishhooks, they become irremovably embedded.

pokemon.name: Feraligatr
pokemon.description: It opens its huge mouth to intimidate enemies. In battle, it runs using its thick and powerful hind legs to charge the foe with incredible speed.

pokemon.name: Mega-Feraligatr
pokemon.description: With its arms and hoodlike fin, this Pokémon forms a gigantic set of jaws with a bite 10 times as powerful as Mega Feraligatr's actual jaws.

pokemon.name: Sentret
pokemon.description: They take turns standing guard when it is time to sleep. The sentry awakens the others if it senses danger. If one becomes separated, it turns sleepless with fear.

pokemon.name: Furret
pokemon.description: A Furret has a very slim build. When under attack, it can squirm through narrow spaces and get away. In spite of its short limbs, it is very nimble and fleet.

pokemon.name: Hoothoot
pokemon.description: It has an internal organ that senses the earth's rotation. Using this special organ, a Hoothoot begins hooting at precisely the same time every day.

pokemon.name: Noctowl
pokemon.description: It unfailingly catches prey in darkness. Noctowl owe their success to superior vision that allows them to see in minimal light, and to their supple and silent wings.

pokemon.name: Ledyba
pokemon.description: Ledyba communicate using a fluid that they secrete from where the legs join the body. They are said to convey feelings to others by altering the fluid's scent.

pokemon.name: Ledian
pokemon.description: It is said that in lands with clean air, where the stars fill the sky, there live many Ledian. For good reason, they use the light of the stars as energy.

pokemon.name: Spinarak
pokemon.description: The web it spins can be considered its second nervous system. It is said that a Spinarak determines its prey by the tiny vibrations it feels through the web.

pokemon.name: Ariados
pokemon.description: Its feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. It constricts its foe with thin and strong silk webbing.

pokemon.name: Chinchou
pokemon.description: When it senses danger, it discharges positive and negative electricity from its two antennae. It lives in depths beyond sunlight's reach.

pokemon.name: Lanturn
pokemon.description: The light-emitting orbs on its back are very bright. They are formed from a part of its dorsal fin. This Pokémon illuminates the inky darkness of deep seas.

pokemon.name: Togepi
pokemon.description: As its energy, it uses the feelings of compassion and pleasure exuded by people and Pokémon. It stores up happy feelings in its shell, then shares them out.

pokemon.name: Togetic
pokemon.description: It is said to be a Pokémon that brings good fortune. When it spots someone who is pure of heart, a Togetic appears and shares its happiness with that person.

pokemon.name: Togekiss
pokemon.description: As everyone knows, it visits peaceful regions, bringing them gifts of kindness and sweet blessings. It will never appear where there is strife.

pokemon.name: Natu
pokemon.description: It runs up short trees that grow on the savanna to peck at new shoots. A Natu's eyes look as if they are always observing something.

pokemon.name: Xatu
pokemon.description: It has the enigmatic power of foreseeing the future. Some people in different lands have long believed that Xatu are emissaries from another world.

pokemon.name: Mareep
pokemon.description: Its fluffy wool rubs together and builds a static charge. The more energy is charged, the more brightly the lightbulb at the tip of its tail glows.

pokemon.name: Flaaffy
pokemon.description: Its fleece quality changes to generate strong static electricity with a small amount of wool. The bare, slick parts of its hide are shielded against electricity.

pokemon.name: Ampharos
pokemon.description: It gives off so much light that it can be seen even from space. People in the old days used its light to send signals back and forth with others far away.

pokemon.name: Mega-Ampharos
pokemon.description: Massive amounts of energy intensely stimulated Ampharos's cells, apparently awakening its long-sleeping dragon's blood.

pokemon.name: Azurill
pokemon.description: Its tail, which is packed with nutrition, is very bouncy like a rubber ball. On sunny days they gather at the edge of water and splash about for fun.

pokemon.name: Marill
pokemon.description: Its body is covered with water-repellent fur. Because of the fur, it can swim through water at high speed without being slowed by the water's resistance.

pokemon.name: Azumarill
pokemon.description: It lives in water virtually all day long. Its body color and pattern act as camouflage that makes it tough for enemies to spot in water.

pokemon.name: Bonsly
pokemon.description: Bonsly prefers an arid atmosphere. In order to adjust the level of fluids in its body, it exudes water from its eyes. This makes it appear to be crying.

pokemon.name: Sudowoodo
pokemon.description: It mimics a tree to avoid being attacked by enemies. But since its forelegs remain green throughout the year, it is easily identified as a fake in the winter.

pokemon.name: Hoppip
pokemon.description: This Pokémon drifts and floats with the wind. If it senses the approach of strong winds, a Hoppip links leaves with others to prepare against being blown away.

pokemon.name: Skiploom
pokemon.description: It blossoms when the temperature rises above 64 degrees F. Because its flower's blooming changes with the temperature, it is sometimes used as a thermometer.

pokemon.name: Jumpluff
pokemon.description: Jumpluff ride warm southern winds to cross the sea and fly to foreign lands. This Pokémon lands when it encounters cold air while it is floating.

pokemon.name: Aipom
pokemon.description: Its tail ends with a dexterous, handlike appendage. However, because it uses the tail so much, Aipom's real hands have become rather clumsy.

pokemon.name: Ambipom
pokemon.description: They live on large trees. Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, Ambipom rarely uses its hands now.

pokemon.name: Sunkern
pokemon.description: Sunkern try to minimize movement to conserve the nutrients they have stored in their bodies for evolution. They will not eat, subsisting only on morning dew.

pokemon.name: Sunflora
pokemon.description: Sunflora convert solar energy into nutrition. They are highly active in the warm daytime but suddenly stop moving as soon as the sun sets.

pokemon.name: Yanma
pokemon.description: It can see 360 degrees without moving its eyes. It is a great flier capable of making sudden stops and turning midair to quickly chase down targeted prey.

pokemon.name: Yanmega
pokemon.description: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. It is adept at biting apart foes while flying by at high speed.

pokemon.name: Wooper
pokemon.description: Wooper usually live in water but come out onto land seeking food occasionally. On land, they coat their bodies with a gooey, toxic film.

pokemon.name: Quagsire
pokemon.description: A Quagsire hunts by leaving its mouth wide open in water and waiting for its prey to blunder in. Because it doesn't move, it does not get very hungry.

pokemon.name: Paldean-Wooper
pokemon.description: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

pokemon.name: Clodsire
pokemon.description: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It's a risky move that puts everything on the line.

pokemon.name: Murkrow
pokemon.description: Murkrow were feared as the alleged bearers of ill fortune. It shows strong interest in anything that sparkles. It will even try to steal rings from women.

pokemon.name: Honchkrow
pokemon.description: Becoming active at night, it is known to swarm with numerous Murkrow in tow. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its cronies.

pokemon.name: Misdreavus
pokemon.description: A Misdreavus frightens people with a creepy, sobbing cry. It apparently uses its red spheres to absorb the fear of foes as its nutrition.

pokemon.name: Mismagius
pokemon.description: Its cries sound like incantations. Those hearing it are tormented by headaches and hallucinations. It appears where you least expect it.

pokemon.name: Unown (A)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is shaped like ancient text characters. Although research is ongoing, it is a mystery as to which came first, the ancient writings or the various Unown.
