pokemon.name: Wynaut
pokemon.description: A Wynaut loves to eat sweet fruits. It cleverly picks fruits using its earlike arms. They gather in fruit gardens, drawn by the fragrance.

pokemon.name: Wobbuffet
pokemon.description: Usually docile, a Wobbuffet strikes back ferociously if its black tail is attacked. It makes its lair in caves where it waits for nightfall.

pokemon.name: Girafarig
pokemon.description: A Girafarig is an herbivore--it eats grass and tree shoots. While it is eating, its tail makes chewing and swallowing motions as if it were also eating.

pokemon.name: Farigiraf
pokemon.description: Now that the brain waves from the head and tail are synced up, the psychic power of this Pokémon is 10 times stronger than Girafarig's.

pokemon.name: Pineco
pokemon.description: A Pineco hangs from a tree branch and waits for prey. While eating, if it is disturbed by someone shaking its tree, it falls on the ground and suddenly explodes.

pokemon.name: Forretress
pokemon.description: It keeps itself inside its steel shell. The shell is opened when it is catching prey, but it is so quick that the shell's inside cannot be seen.

pokemon.name: Dunsparce
pokemon.description: Its drill-tipped tail is used to burrow into the ground backwards. This Pokémon is known to make its nest in complex shapes deep under the ground.

pokemon.name: Dudunsparce-Two-Segments
pokemon.description: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

pokemon.name: Dudunsparce-Three-Segments
pokemon.description: A recent study uncovered that the number of segments a Dudunsparce's body has is determined by the Pokémon's genes.

pokemon.name: Gligar
pokemon.description: It glides without making a single sound. It grasps the face of its foe using its hind and large front claws, then stabs with its poison barb.

pokemon.name: Gliscor
pokemon.description: Gliscor observes prey while hanging upside down from branches. Its flight is soundless. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

pokemon.name: Snubbull
pokemon.description: By baring its fangs and making a scary face, it sends smaller Pokémon scurrying in terror. The Snubbull does seem a little sad at making its foes flee.

pokemon.name: Granbull
pokemon.description: It has a particularly well-developed lower jaw. The huge fangs are heavy, causing it to tilt its head. Unless it is startled, it will not try to bite.

pokemon.name: Qwilfish
pokemon.description: A Qwilfish uses the pressure of water it swallows to shoot toxic quills all at once from all over its body. It finds swimming to be somewhat challenging.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Qwilfish
pokemon.description: A form of Qwilfish from the distant past. Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere.

pokemon.name: Overqwil
pokemon.description: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname “Sea Fiend”. It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

pokemon.name: Shuckle
pokemon.description: A Shuckle hides under rocks, keeping its body concealed inside its shell while eating stored berries. The berries mix with its body fluids to become a juice.

pokemon.name: Heracross
pokemon.description: They gather in forests seeking the sweet sap of trees. It is completely clad in a steel-hard shell. It is proud of its horn, which it uses to fling foes.

pokemon.name: Mega-Heracross
pokemon.description: A tremendous influx of energy builds it up, but when Mega Evolution ends, Heracross is bothered by terrible soreness in its muscles.

pokemon.name: Sneasel
pokemon.description: A Sneasel scales trees by punching its hooked claws into the bark. It seeks out unguarded nests and steals eggs for food while the parents are away.

pokemon.name: Weavile
pokemon.description: They travel in groups of four or five, leaving signs for one another on trees and rocks. They bring down their prey with coordinated attacks.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Sneasel
pokemon.description: Its sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught.

pokemon.name: Sneasler
pokemon.description: Because of its wicked poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on frozen highlands. It prefers solitude and doesn't form packs.

pokemon.name: Teddiursa
pokemon.description: It licks its palms that are sweetened by being soaked in honey. A Teddiursa makes its own honey by blending fruits and pollen collected by Beedrill.

pokemon.name: Ursaring
pokemon.description: In forests, it is said that there are many streams and towering trees where an Ursaring gathers food. It walks through its forest collecting food every day.

pokemon.name: Ursaluna
pokemon.description: Swampy terrain gives Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

pokemon.name: Bloodmoon-Ursaluna
pokemon.description: It crossed the sea and drifted ashore in a new land. Surviving in this place led it to take on a unique appearance and gain special powers.

pokemon.name: Slugma
pokemon.description: It is a species of Pokémon that lives in volcanic areas. If its body cools, its skin hardens and immobilizes it. To avoid that, it sleeps near magma.

pokemon.name: Magcargo
pokemon.description: The shell on its back is made of hardened magma. Tens of thousands of years spent living in volcanic craters have turned Magcargo's bodies into magma.

pokemon.name: Swinub
pokemon.description: It roots for food by rubbing its snout against the ground. Its favorite food is a mushroom that grows under dried grass. It occasionally roots out hot springs.

pokemon.name: Piloswine
pokemon.description: A Piloswine is covered by a thick coat of long hair for enduring freezing cold. It uses its tusks to dig up food that has been buried under ice.

pokemon.name: Mamoswine
pokemon.description: A frozen Mamoswine was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. It woke up to much amazement. This Pokémon has been around for a long, long, long time.

pokemon.name: Corsola
pokemon.description: Corsola live in warm southern seas. If the sea becomes polluted, the beautiful coral stalks become discolored and crumble away in tatters.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Corsola
pokemon.description: Sudden climate change wiped out this ancient kind of Corsola. With its branches, it absorbs others' life-force. It curses those unaware that kick it by accident.

pokemon.name: Cursola
pokemon.description: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon's core spirit.

pokemon.name: Remoraid
pokemon.description: A Remoraid uses its abdominal muscles to forcefully expel swallowed water, then shoot down flying prey. When evolution approaches, it travels down rivers.

pokemon.name: Octillery
pokemon.description: It ensnares its foe with its suction- cupped tentacles before delivering the finishing blow. If the foe turns out to be too strong, it spews ink to escape.

pokemon.name: Delibird
pokemon.description: It carries food bundled up in its tail. There was a famous explorer who managed to scale Mt. Everest thanks to a Delibird sharing its food.

pokemon.name: Mantyke
pokemon.description: When it swims close to the surface of the ocean, people aboard ships are able to observe the pattern on its back. It often swims in a school of Remoraid.

pokemon.name: Mantine
pokemon.description: On sunny days, schools of Mantine can be seen elegantly leaping over the waves. It is not bothered by the Remoraid that hitches rides.

pokemon.name: Skarmory
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that has a body and wings of steel. People in the past used feathers fallen from Skarmory to make swords and knives.

pokemon.name: Mega-Skarmory
pokemon.description: Due to the effects of Mega Evolution, its pincers have taken a more diabolical form, ripping anything they pierce to shreds.

pokemon.name: Houndour
pokemon.description: Houndour communicate with each other using a variety of cries to corner their prey. This Pokémon's remarkable teamwork is simply unparalleled.

pokemon.name: Houndoom
pokemon.description: In a Houndoom pack, the one with its horns raked sharply back serves a leadership role. They choose their leader by fighting among themselves.

pokemon.name: Mega-Houndoom
pokemon.description: Its red claws and the tips of its tail are melting from high internal temperatures that are painful to Houndoom itself.

pokemon.name: Phanpy
pokemon.description: Phanpy's big ears serve as broad fans. When it becomes hot, it flaps the ears busily to cool down. Even the young are very strong.

pokemon.name: Donphan
pokemon.description: A Donphan is so strong it can easily haul a dump truck. Its hide has toughened to a rock-hard state. An ordinary sort of attack won't even leave a scratch.

pokemon.name: Stantler
pokemon.description: Stantler's magnificent antlers were once traded at high prices as works of art. As a result, this Pokémon was hunted close to extinction.

pokemon.name: Wyrdeer
pokemon.description: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when it is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat and is useful for winter clothing.

pokemon.name: Smeargle
pokemon.description: A Smeargle marks its territory using a fluid that leaks out from the tip of its tail. About 5,000 different marks left by this Pokémon have been found.

pokemon.name: Miltank
pokemon.description: It gives over five gallons of milk daily. Its sweet milk is enjoyed by children and grown-ups alike. People who can't drink milk turn it into yogurt and eat it instead.

pokemon.name: Raikou
pokemon.description: Raikou embodies the speed of lightning. Its roars send shock waves shuddering through the air and ground as if lightning bolts were crashing down.

pokemon.name: Entei
pokemon.description: Entei embodies the passion of magma. It is thought to have been born in the eruption of a volcano. It blasts fire that consumes all that it touches.

pokemon.name: Suicune
pokemon.description: Suicune embodies the compassion of a pure spring of water. It runs across the land with gliding elegance. It has the power to purify dirty water.

pokemon.name: Larvitar
pokemon.description: A Larvitar is born deep under the ground. It must eat its way through the soil above and reach the surface for it to see its parents' faces.

pokemon.name: Pupitar
pokemon.description: A Pupitar creates a gas inside its body that it ejects under compression to propel itself like a jet. Its body can withstand a collision with solid steel.

pokemon.name: Tyranitar
pokemon.description: A Tyranitar is so overwhelmingly powerful, it can bring down a whole mountain to make its nest. It roams in mountains seeking new opponents to fight.

pokemon.name: Mega-Tyranitar
pokemon.description: Due to the colossal power poured into it, this Pokémon's back split right open. Its destructive instincts are the only thing keeping it moving.

pokemon.name: Lugia
pokemon.description: Lugia is so powerful even a light fluttering of its wings can blow apart houses. As a result, it chooses to live out of sight deep under the sea.

pokemon.name: Ho-Oh
pokemon.description: Its feathers--which glow in seven colors depending on the angle at which they are struck by light--are thought to bring joy. It is said to live at the foot of a rainbow.

pokemon.name: Celebi
pokemon.description: This Pokémon came from the future by crossing over time. It is thought that so long as Celebi appears, a bright and shining future awaits us.

pokemon.name: Treecko
pokemon.description: It makes its nest in a giant tree in the forest. It ferociously guards against anything nearing its territory. It is said to be the protector of the forest's trees.

pokemon.name: Grovyle
pokemon.description: Leaves grow out of this Pokémon's body. They help obscure a Grovyle from the eyes of its enemies while it is in a thickly overgrown forest.

pokemon.name: Sceptile
pokemon.description: In the jungle, its power is without equal. This Pokémon carefully grows trees and plants. It regulates its body temperature by basking in sunlight.

pokemon.name: Mega-Sceptile
pokemon.description: Thanks to the power in its quick legs, Mega Sceptile can be on its opponent in a flash. It can cut off a portion of its tail to fire it like a missile at an opponent.

pokemon.name: Torchic
pokemon.description: If attacked, it strikes back by spitting balls of fire it forms in its stomach. A Torchic dislikes darkness because it can't see its surroundings.

pokemon.name: Combusken
pokemon.description: It lashes out with 10 kicks per second. Its strong fighting instinct compels it to keep up its offensive until the opponent gives up.

pokemon.name: Blaziken
pokemon.description: It learns martial arts that use punches and kicks. Every several years, its old feathers burn off, and new, supple feathers grow back in their place.

pokemon.name: Mega-Blaziken
pokemon.description: As it unleashes a flurry of savage kicks, its legs can begin to burn from the friction of the surrounding atmosphere. They're always a source of pride to it.

pokemon.name: Mudkip
pokemon.description: On land, it can powerfully lift large boulders by planting its four feet and heaving. It sleeps by burying itself in soil at the water's edge.

pokemon.name: Marshtomp
pokemon.description: Its toughened hind legs enable it to stand upright. Because it weakens if its skin dries out, it replenishes fluids by playing in mud.

pokemon.name: Swampert
pokemon.description: If it senses the approach of a storm and a tidal wave, it protects its seaside nest by piling up boulders. It swims as fast as a jet ski.

pokemon.name: Mega-Swampert
pokemon.description: When it Mega Evolves, the strength that it needs to act in the water is increased. It can use its tenacious power both on land and in the water.

pokemon.name: Poochyena
pokemon.description: It savagely threatens foes with bared fangs. It chases after fleeing targets tenaciously. It turns tail and runs, however, if the foe strikes back.

pokemon.name: Mightyena
pokemon.description: In the wild, Mightyena live in a pack. They never defy their leader's orders. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

pokemon.name: Zigzagoon
pokemon.description: Rubbing its nose against the ground, it always wanders about back and forth in search of something. It is distinguished by the zigzag footprints it leaves.

pokemon.name: Linoone
pokemon.description: It is exceedingly fast if it only has to run in a straight line. When it spots pond- dwelling prey underwater, it quickly leaps in and catches it with its sharp claws.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Zigzagoon
pokemon.description: Its restlessness has it constantly moving in zigzags. It will purposely run into other Pokémon to start fights. It's thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Linoone
pokemon.description: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself. It uses its long tongue to taunt them to then tackle forcefully.

pokemon.name: Obstagoon
pokemon.description: Its voice is staggering in volume. Obstagoon has a tendency to take on a threatening posture and shout--this move is known as Obstruct.

pokemon.name: Wurmple
pokemon.description: It sticks to tree branches and eats leaves. The thread it spits from its mouth, which becomes gooey when it touches air, slows the movement of its foes.

pokemon.name: Silcoon
pokemon.description: It prepares for evolution using the energy it stored while it was a Wurmple. It keeps watch over the surroundings with its two eyes.

pokemon.name: Beautifly
pokemon.description: Its colorfully patterned wings are its most prominent feature. It flies through flower-covered fields collecting pollen. It attacks ferociously when angered.

pokemon.name: Cascoon
pokemon.description: To avoid detection by its enemies, it hides motionlessly beneath large leaves and in the gaps of branches. It also attaches dead leaves to its body for camouflage.

pokemon.name: Dustox
pokemon.description: It is a nocturnal Pokémon that flies from fields and mountains to the attraction of streetlights at night. It looses highly toxic powder from its wings.

pokemon.name: Lotad
pokemon.description: This Pokémon lives in ponds with clean water. It is known to ferry small Pokémon across ponds by carrying them on the broad leaf on its head.

pokemon.name: Lombre
pokemon.description: In the evening, it takes great delight in popping out of rivers and startling people. It feeds on aquatic moss that grows on rocks in the riverbed.

pokemon.name: Ludicolo
pokemon.description: When it hears festive music, all the cells in its body become stimulated, and it begins moving in rhythm. It does not quail even when it faces a tough opponent.

pokemon.name: Seedot
pokemon.description: It hangs off branches and absorbs nutrients. When it finishes eating, its body becomes so heavy that it drops to the ground with a thump.

pokemon.name: Nuzleaf
pokemon.description: A forest-dwelling Pokémon that is skilled at climbing trees. Its long and pointed nose is its weak point. It loses power if the nose is gripped.

pokemon.name: Shiftry
pokemon.description: It is said to arrive on chilly, wintry winds. Feared from long ago as the guardian of forests, this Pokémon lives in a deep forest where people do not venture.

pokemon.name: Taillow
pokemon.description: Although it is small, it is very courageous. It will take on a larger Skarmory on an equal footing. However, its will weakens if it becomes hungry.

pokemon.name: Swellow
pokemon.description: A Swellow dives upon prey from far above. It never misses its targets. It takes to the skies in search of lands with a warm climate.

pokemon.name: Wingull
pokemon.description: It makes its nest on a sheer cliff at the edge of the sea. It has trouble keeping its wings flapping in flight. Instead, it soars on updrafts.

pokemon.name: Pelipper
pokemon.description: It skims the tops of waves as it flies. When it spots prey, it uses its large beak to scoop up the victim with water. It protects its eggs in its beak.

pokemon.name: Ralts
pokemon.description: A Ralts has the power to sense the emotions of people and Pokémon with the horns on its head. It takes cover if it senses any hostility.

pokemon.name: Kirlia
pokemon.description: A Kirlia has the psychic power to create a rip in the dimensions and see into the future. It is said to dance with pleasure on sunny mornings.

pokemon.name: Gardevoir
pokemon.description: It apparently does not feel the pull of gravity because it supports itself with psychic power. It will give its life to protect its Trainer.

pokemon.name: Mega-Gardevoir
pokemon.description: By opening its heart, Mega Gardevoir becomes able to use tremendous psychic power. The red plate in its chest is said to be a physical manifestation of its heart.

pokemon.name: Gallade
pokemon.description: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows when trying to protect someone. It can sense what its foe is thinking.

pokemon.name: Mega-Gallade
pokemon.description: Mega Gallade has a knightly appearance. It can use its psychic power to reshape both arms into blades simultaneously and fight using a dual-wielding style.

pokemon.name: Surskit
pokemon.description: They gather on puddles after evening downpours, gliding across the surface of water as if sliding. It secretes honey with a sweet aroma from its head.

pokemon.name: Masquerain
pokemon.description: It intimidates foes with the large eyelike patterns on its antennae. Because it can't fly if its wings get wet, it shelters itself from rain under large trees and eaves.

pokemon.name: Shroomish
pokemon.description: It loves to eat damp, composted soil in forests. If you enter a forest after a long rain, you can see many Shroomish feasting on composted soil.

pokemon.name: Breloom
pokemon.description: It scatters spores from holes in the cap on its head. It loves warm and humid climates. It feeds on trees and plants in fields and forests.

pokemon.name: Slakoth
pokemon.description: It sleeps virtually all day and night long. It doesn't change its nest its entire life, but it sometimes travels great distances by swimming in rivers.

pokemon.name: Vigoroth
pokemon.description: It can't keep still because its blood boils with energy. It runs through the fields and mountains all day to calm itself. If it doesn't, it can't sleep at night.

pokemon.name: Slaking
pokemon.description: Hordes of Slaking gather around trees when fruits come into season. They wait around patiently for ripened fruits to fall out of the trees.

pokemon.name: Nincada
pokemon.description: It makes its nest at the roots of a mighty tree. Using its whiskerlike antennae, it probes its surroundings in the pitch-black darkness of soil.

pokemon.name: Ninjask
pokemon.description: Because it darts about vigorously at high speed, it is very difficult to see. Hearing its distinctive cries for too long induces a headache.

pokemon.name: Shedinja
pokemon.description: A peculiar Pokémon that floats in air even though its wings remain completely still. The inside of its body is hollow and utterly dark.

pokemon.name: Whismur
pokemon.description: Its cries equal a jet plane in volume. It inhales through its ear canals. Because of this system, it can cry continually without having to catch its breath.

pokemon.name: Loudred
pokemon.description: It positions the round speakers on its head to assail foes with ultrasonic waves at massive volume. It builds power by stomping the ground.

pokemon.name: Exploud
pokemon.description: It has sound-generating organs all over its body. It communicates with others by adjusting the tone and volume of the cries it emits.

pokemon.name: Makuhita
pokemon.description: It loves to toughen up its body above all else. If you hear quaking rumbles in a cave, it is the sound of Makuhita undertaking strenuous training.

pokemon.name: Hariyama
pokemon.description: It has the habit of challenging others without hesitation to tests of strength. It's been known to stand on train tracks and stop trains using forearm thrusts.

pokemon.name: Nosepass
pokemon.description: Its body emits a powerful magnetism. It feeds on prey that is pulled in by the force. Its magnetism is stronger in cold seasons.

pokemon.name: Probopass
pokemon.description: It freely controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force. With them, it can attack its foes from three directions.

pokemon.name: Skitty
pokemon.description: A Skitty's adorably cute behavior makes it highly popular. In battle, it makes its tail puff out. It threatens foes with a sharp growl.

pokemon.name: Delcatty
pokemon.description: Rather than keeping a permanent lair, it habitually seeks comfortable spots and sleeps there. It is nocturnal and becomes active at dusk.

pokemon.name: Sableye
pokemon.description: It digs branching holes in caves using its sharp claws in search of food--raw gems. A Sableye lurks in darkness and is seen only rarely.

pokemon.name: Mega-Sableye
pokemon.description: Bathed in the energy of Mega Evolution, the gemstone on its chest expands, rips through its skin, and falls out.

pokemon.name: Mawile
pokemon.description: Its giant jaws are actually steel horns that transformed. It fools foes into complacency with its adorable gestures, then chomps them with its huge jaws.

pokemon.name: Mega-Mawile
pokemon.description: Its two sets of jaws thrash about violently as if they each had a will of their own. One gnash from them can turn a boulder to dust.

pokemon.name: Aron
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that is clad in steel armor. A new suit of armor is made when it evolves. The old, discarded armor is salvaged as metal for making iron products.

pokemon.name: Lairon
pokemon.description: When two Lairon meet in the wild, they fight for territory by bashing into each other with their steel bodies. The sound of their collision carries for miles.

pokemon.name: Aggron
pokemon.description: Its iron horns grow longer a little at a time. They are used to determine the Aggron's age. The gouges in its armor are worn with pride as mementos from battles.

pokemon.name: Mega-Aggron
pokemon.description: When Aggron Mega Evolves into Mega Aggron, the destructive ability of its fearsome horns can turn a steel-clad tank into so much scrap metal.

pokemon.name: Meditite
pokemon.description: It continually meditates for hours every day. As a result of rigorous and dedicated yoga training, it has tempered its spiritual power so much it can fly.

pokemon.name: Medicham
pokemon.description: Through crushingly harsh yoga training, it gained the power to foretell its foe's actions. It battles with elegant, dance- like movement.

pokemon.name: Mega-Medicham
pokemon.description: It uses its amped-up willpower to create additional arms for itself. The more it has trained its spirit, the more realistic and dexterous these arms become.

pokemon.name: Electrike
pokemon.description: It generates electricity using friction from the atmosphere. In seasons with especially arid air, its entire body blazes with violent showers of sparks.

pokemon.name: Manectric
pokemon.description: Because lightning falls in their vicinities, Manectric were thought to have been born from lightning. In battle, they create thunderclouds.

pokemon.name: Mega-Manectric
pokemon.description: Too much electricity has built up in its body, irritating Manectric. Its explosive speed is equal to that of a lightning bolt.

pokemon.name: Plusle
pokemon.description: It has the trait of cheering on its fellow Pokémon. By shorting out the electricity it releases from its paws, it creates pom-poms for cheering.

pokemon.name: Minun
pokemon.description: At a meeting of Pokémon academics, it was announced that simultaneous exposure to electricity from a Plusle and Minun will promote circulation and boost vitality.

pokemon.name: Volbeat
pokemon.description: With their taillights lit, Volbeat fly in a swarm, drawing geometric designs in the night sky. They move their nests if their pond water becomes dirty.

pokemon.name: Illumise
pokemon.description: A nocturnal Pokémon that becomes active upon nightfall. It leads a Volbeat swarm to draw patterns in the night sky. Over 200 different patterns have been confirmed.

pokemon.name: Budew
pokemon.description: Over the winter, it closes its bud and endures the cold. Sensitive to changing temperature, the bud is said to bloom when it feels the sun's warm touch.

pokemon.name: Roselia
pokemon.description: A Roselia that drinks nutritionally rich springwater blooms with lovely flowers. The fragrance of its flowers has the effect of making its foes careless.

pokemon.name: Roserade
pokemon.description: Its sweet aroma attracts prey. With the movements of a dancer, it strikes with whips that are densely lined with poison thorns.

pokemon.name: Gulpin
pokemon.description: This Pokémon's stomach fluid can even digest scrap iron. In one gulp, it can swallow something that is as large as itself.

pokemon.name: Swalot
pokemon.description: Its powerful stomach acid is capable of digesting almost anything. The one thing in the whole world a Swalot can't digest is its own stomach.

pokemon.name: Carvanha
pokemon.description: Carvanha attack ships in swarms, making them sink. Although it is said to be a very vicious Pokémon, it timidly flees as soon as it finds itself alone.

pokemon.name: Sharpedo
pokemon.description: The vicious and sly gangster of the sea. Its skin is specially textured to minimize drag in water. Its speed tops out at over 75 miles per hour.

pokemon.name: Mega-Sharpedo
pokemon.description: The yellow patterns it bears are old scars. The energy from Mega Evolution runs through them, causing it sharp pain and suffering.

pokemon.name: Wailmer
pokemon.description: While this Pokémon usually lives in the sea, it can survive on land, although not too long. It loses vitality if its body becomes dried out.

pokemon.name: Wailord
pokemon.description: It breathes through nostrils that it raises above the sea. By inhaling to its maximum capacity, a Wailord can dive close to 10,000 feet beneath the waves.

pokemon.name: Numel
pokemon.description: A Numel stores boiling magma in the hump on its back. It is a hardy Pokémon that can transport a 220-pound load. It has served humans at work since long ago.

pokemon.name: Camerupt
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that lives in the crater of a volcano. Every 10 years, the volcanoes on its back erupt violently. Research is under way on the cause of eruption.

pokemon.name: Mega-Camerupt
pokemon.description: The humps on its back have transformed into one large and very active volcano. It has a deep hatred of water or any moisture and has an explosive personality.

pokemon.name: Torkoal
pokemon.description: It battles using energy it gets from burning coal. When loosing smoke from its nostrils, it lets off a sound that is similar to a locomotive's horn.

pokemon.name: Spoink
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that manipulates psychic power at will. It doesn't stop bouncing even when it is asleep. It loves eating mushrooms that grow underground.

pokemon.name: Grumpig
pokemon.description: It stores power in the black pearls on its forehead. When it uses psychic power, it performs an odd dance step. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

pokemon.name: Spinda
pokemon.description: It is distinguished by a pattern of spots that is always different. Its unsteady, tottering walk has the effect of fouling its foe's aim.

pokemon.name: Trapinch
pokemon.description: Its big jaws crunch through boulders. Because its head is so big, it has a hard time getting back upright if it tips over onto its back.

pokemon.name: Vibrava
pokemon.description: It looses ultrasonic waves by rubbing its wings together. Since a Vibrava's wings are still in the process of growing, it can only fly short distances.

pokemon.name: Flygon
pokemon.description: The flapping of its wings sounds like singing. To prevent detection by enemies, it hides itself by flapping up a cloud of desert sand.

pokemon.name: Cacnea
pokemon.description: Cacnea live in deserts with virtually no rainfall. It battles by swinging its thick, spiked arms. Once a year, a yellow flower blooms.

pokemon.name: Cacturne
pokemon.description: After spending thousands of years in harsh deserts, its blood transformed into the same substances as sand. It is nocturnal, so it hunts at night.

pokemon.name: Swablu
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that has wings like cottony clouds. After enduring winter, in which little food is available, Swablu flocks move closer to towns in the spring.

pokemon.name: Altaria
pokemon.description: It hums in a beautiful soprano voice. It flies among white clouds in the blue sky. It launches intensely hot fireballs from its mouth.

pokemon.name: Mega-Altaria
pokemon.description: It sings with a voice that is even more beautiful than before it Mega Evolved. Its feathers became larger for protection and glimmer with an iridescent sheen.

pokemon.name: Zangoose
pokemon.description: When it battles, it stands on its hind legs and attacks with its sharply clawed forelegs. Its fur bristles if it encounters any Seviper.

pokemon.name: Seviper
pokemon.description: Seviper and Zangoose are eternal rivals. It counters a Zangoose's dazzling agility with its swordlike tail, which also oozes a horrible poison.

pokemon.name: Lunatone
pokemon.description: It becomes very active on the night of a full moon. This Pokémon was first discovered 40 years ago at the site of a meteor strike.

pokemon.name: Solrock
pokemon.description: Solar energy is the source of this Pokémon's power. On sunny days, groups of Solrock line up facing the sun and absorb its light.

pokemon.name: Barboach
pokemon.description: Its body is covered with a slimy film. The film acts as a barrier to prevent germs in muddy water from entering the Barboach's body.

pokemon.name: Whiscash
pokemon.description: Mysteriously, it can foretell earthquakes. In the daytime, it sleeps in mud at the bottom of a pond. When it awakens, it continually feeds throughout the night.

pokemon.name: Corphish
pokemon.description: Once it grips prey with its large pincers, it will never let go, no matter what. It is a hardy Pokémon that can thrive in any environment.

pokemon.name: Crawdaunt
pokemon.description: A brutish Pokémon that loves to battle. A veteran Crawdaunt that has prevailed in hundreds of battles has giant pincers marked with countless scars.

pokemon.name: Baltoy
pokemon.description: A Baltoy moves by spinning on its single foot. It has been depicted in murals adorning the walls of a once-bustling city in an ancient age.

pokemon.name: Claydol
pokemon.description: A Claydol sleeps while hovering in midair. Its arms are separate from its body. They are kept floating by the Pokémon's manipulation of psychic power.

pokemon.name: Lileep
pokemon.description: It disguises itself as seaweed by making its tentacles sway. Unsuspecting prey that come too close are swallowed whole. It became extinct 100 million years ago.

pokemon.name: Cradily
pokemon.description: It drags its heavy body along the seafloor. It makes its nest in the shallows of warm seas. Cradily can be seen on beaches when the tide goes out.

pokemon.name: Anorith
pokemon.description: It was resurrected from a fossil using the power of science. It swims by undulating the eight wings at its sides. They were feet that adapted to life in the sea.

pokemon.name: Armaldo
pokemon.description: Armaldo usually lives on land. However, when it hunts for prey, it dives beneath the ocean. It swims around using its two large wings.

pokemon.name: Feebas
pokemon.description: Feebas live in ponds that are heavily infested with weeds. Because of its hopelessly shabby appearance, it seems as if few Trainers raise it.

pokemon.name: Milotic
pokemon.description: It is said to live at the bottom of large lakes. Considered to be the most beautiful of all Pokémon, it has been depicted in paintings and statues.

pokemon.name: Castform (Normal)
pokemon.description: It alters its form depending on the weather. Changes in the climate such as the temperature and humidity appear to affect its cellular structure.

pokemon.name: Castform (Sunny)
pokemon.description: Castform takes this form on bright days. In an experiment where is was placed in front of a heater, it didn't change to this form. Its body is warm and toasty.

pokemon.name: Castform (Rainy)
pokemon.description: This is Castform's form while it's raining. In an experiment where it was placed in a shower, it didn't change to this form. Its body is soft, slightly swollen with water.

pokemon.name: Castform (Snowy)
pokemon.description: This is Castform's form in a hailstorm. In an experiment where it was placed in a freezer, it didn't change to this form. Its cold skin is as smooth as ice.

pokemon.name: Kecleon
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that has the ability to alter its body colors to match its surroundings. A Kecleon reverts to its original colors if it is startled.

pokemon.name: Shuppet
pokemon.description: This Pokémon roams about deep in the night seeking such negative emotions as grudges and envy. It retreats to its nest when the sun begins to rise.

pokemon.name: Banette
pokemon.description: An abandoned plush doll became this Pokémon. They are said to live in garbage dumps and wander about in search of the children that threw them away.

pokemon.name: Mega-Banette
pokemon.description: Extraordinary energy amplifies its cursing power to such an extent that it can't help but curse its own Trainer.

pokemon.name: Duskull
pokemon.description: A glare from its single scarlet eye makes even burly grown-ups freeze in utter fear. It is a nocturnal Pokémon that roams about under the cloak of darkness.

pokemon.name: Dusclops
pokemon.description: It is thought that its body is hollow with only a spectral ball of fire burning inside. However, no one has been able to confirm this theory as fact.

pokemon.name: Dusknoir
pokemon.description: This feared Pokémon is said to travel to worlds unknown. Some even believe that it takes lost spirits into its pliant body and guides them home.

pokemon.name: Tropius
pokemon.description: It flies by flapping its broad leaves. The bunch of fruit that grows around its neck is deliciously sweet. In the spring, it scatters pollen from its neck.

pokemon.name: Chingling
pokemon.description: There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound. It can cry for an awfully long time.

pokemon.name: Chimecho
pokemon.description: They fly about very actively when the hot season arrives. They communicate among themselves using seven different and distinguishing cries.

pokemon.name: Mega-Chimecho
pokemon.description: It vibrates its entire body to attack in all directions using sound waves. Any who hear the vibrations get so confused, they forget who they are.

pokemon.name: Absol
pokemon.description: It sharply senses even subtle changes in the sky and the land to predict natural disasters. It is a long-lived Pokémon that has a life-span of 100 years.

pokemon.name: Mega-Absol
pokemon.description: As the energy of Mega Evolution fills it, its fur bristles. What you see on its back are not true wings, and this Pokémon isn't able to fly.

pokemon.name: Mega-Absol-Z
pokemon.description: Using fur that it has made into sharp, clawlike shapes, it cuts down foes with a single blow. This is an act of kindness to keep them from suffering.

pokemon.name: Snorunt
pokemon.description: They tend to move about in groups of around five Snorunt. In snowy regions, it is said that when they are seen late at night, snowfall will arrive by morning.

pokemon.name: Glalie
pokemon.description: A Glalie has the power to instantaneously freeze moisture in the atmosphere. A dazzling cloud of diamondlike ice crystals forms around its body.

pokemon.name: Mega-Glalie
pokemon.description: The power of Mega Evolution was so strong that it smashed Glalie's jaw. Its inability to eat very well leaves Glalie irritated.

pokemon.name: Froslass
pokemon.description: When it finds people or Pokémon it likes, it freezes them and takes them to its chilly den, where they become its decorations.

pokemon.name: Mega-Froslass
pokemon.description: This Pokémon can use eerie cold air imbued with ghost energy to freeze even insubstantial things, such as flames or the wind.

pokemon.name: Spheal
pokemon.description: It is completely covered with plushy fur. As a result, it never feels the cold even when it is rolling about on ice floes or diving in the sea.

pokemon.name: Sealeo
pokemon.description: Sealeo live in herds on ice floes. Using its powerful flippers, it shatters ice. It dives into the sea to hunt prey five times a day.

pokemon.name: Walrein
pokemon.description: To protect its herd, the leader battles anything that invades its territory, even at the cost of its life. Its tusks may snap off in battle.

pokemon.name: Clamperl
pokemon.description: A Clamperl slams its shell closed on prey to prevent escape. The pearl it creates upon evolution is said to be infused with a mysterious energy.

pokemon.name: Huntail
pokemon.description: To withstand the crushing pressure of water deep under the sea, its spine is very thick and sturdy. Its tail, which is shaped like a small fish, has eyes that light up.

pokemon.name: Gorebyss
pokemon.description: A Gorebyss siphons the body fluids of prey through its thin, tubular mouth. Its light pink body color turns vivid when it finishes feeding.

pokemon.name: Relicanth
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that was once believed to have been extinct. The species has not changed its form for 100 million years. It walks on the seafloor using its pectoral fins.

pokemon.name: Luvdisc
pokemon.description: Luvdisc make the branches of Corsola their nests. There is a custom from long ago of giving a Luvdisc as a gift to express one's feelings of love.

pokemon.name: Bagon
pokemon.description: Although it is small, this Pokémon is very powerful because its body is a bundle of muscles. It launches head-butts with its ironlike skull.

pokemon.name: Shelgon
pokemon.description: It hardly eats while it awaits evolution. It becomes hardier by enduring hunger. Its shell peels off the instant it begins to evolve.

pokemon.name: Salamence
pokemon.description: After many long years, its cellular structure underwent a sudden mutation to grow wings. When angered, it loses all thought and rampages out of control.

pokemon.name: Mega-Salamence
pokemon.description: Mega Evolution fuels its brutality, and it may even turn on the Trainer who raised it. It's been dubbed the blood-soaked. crescent.

pokemon.name: Beldum
pokemon.description: When Beldum gather in a swarm, they move in perfect unison as if they were but one Pokémon. They communicate with each other using brain waves.

pokemon.name: Metang
pokemon.description: The claws tipping its arms pack the destructive power to tear through thick iron sheets as if they were silk. It flies at over 60 miles per hour.

pokemon.name: Metagross
pokemon.description: Metagross has four brains that are joined by a complex neural network. As a result of integration, this Pokémon is smarter than a supercomputer.

pokemon.name: Mega-Metagross
pokemon.description: When it knows it can't win, it digs the claws on its legs into its opponent and starts the countdown to a big explosion.

pokemon.name: Regirock
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that is made entirely of rocks and boulders. If parts of its body chip off in battle, Regirock repairs itself by adding new rocks.

pokemon.name: Regice
pokemon.description: Its entire body is made of Antarctic ice. After extensive studies, researchers believe the ice was formed during an ice age.

pokemon.name: Registeel
pokemon.description: Its body is harder than any other kind of metal. The body metal is composed of a mysterious substance. Not only is it hard, it shrinks and stretches flexibly.

pokemon.name: Latias
pokemon.description: They make a small herd of only several members. They rarely make contact with people or other Pokémon. They disappear if they sense enemies.

pokemon.name: Mega-Latias
pokemon.description: Its body is smaller than Mega Latios's body. It is more agile and can make very sharp turns. When it Mega Evolves, its defensive strength grows substantially.

pokemon.name: Latios
pokemon.description: Even in hiding, it can detect the locations of others and sense their emotions since it has telepathy. Its intelligence allows it to understand human languages.

pokemon.name: Mega-Latios
pokemon.description: It's larger than Mega Latias, and can achieve higher speeds in flight. This Pokémon can use its speed in battle to unleash a flurry of attacks.

pokemon.name: Kyogre
pokemon.description: Kyogre has appeared in mythology as the creator of the sea. After long years of feuding with Groudon, it took to sleep at the bottom of the sea.

pokemon.name: Primal-Kyogre
pokemon.description: When Kyogre roared, water poured forth and the seas spread outward. Dark clouds enshrouded the world, and the deluge fell upon all…

pokemon.name: Groudon
pokemon.description: Groudon has appeared in mythology as the creator of the land. It sleeps in magma underground and is said to make volcanoes erupt on awakening.

pokemon.name: Primal-Groudon
pokemon.description: When Groudon howled, the earth swelled and the land grew wide. The sun blazed atop and all the world around the creature was enveloped in incandescent heat.

pokemon.name: Rayquaza
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that flies endlessly in the ozone layer. It is said it would descend to the ground if Kyogre and Groudon were to fight.

pokemon.name: Mega-Rayquaza
pokemon.description: Particles stream from the filaments that extend from its jaw. They can control the density and humidity of the air, allowing Rayquaza to manipulate the weather.

pokemon.name: Jirachi
pokemon.description: Jirachi is said to make wishes come true. While it sleeps, a tough crystalline shell envelops the body to protect it from enemies.

pokemon.name: Deoxys
pokemon.description: Deoxys emerged from a virus that came from space. It is highly intelligent and can shoot lasers from the crystalline organ on its chest.

pokemon.name: Deoxys (Attack)
pokemon.description: This Deoxys has transformed into its aggressive guise. It can fool enemies by altering its appearance.

pokemon.name: Deoxys (Defense)
pokemon.description: When it changes form, an aurora appears. It absorbs attacks by altering its cellular structure.

pokemon.name: Deoxys (Speed)
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that mutated from an extraterrestrial virus exposed to a laser beam. Its body is configured for superior agility and speed.

pokemon.name: Turtwig
pokemon.description: The shell on its back is made of soil. On a very healthy Turtwig, the shell should feel moist. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

pokemon.name: Grotle
pokemon.description: A Grotle that lives in the forest is said to have its own secret springwater well. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.

pokemon.name: Torterra
pokemon.description: Some Pokémon are born on a Torterra's back and spend their entire life there. Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground, a gigantic Torterra dwelled.

pokemon.name: Chimchar
pokemon.description: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Before going to sleep, Chimchar extinguish the flame on their tails to prevent fires.

pokemon.name: Monferno
pokemon.description: It carefully controls the intensity of the flame on its tail, so as to keep its foes at an adequate distance. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

pokemon.name: Infernape
pokemon.description: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It uses a special kind of martial arts involving all of its limbs to take on any opponent.

pokemon.name: Piplup
pokemon.description: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care. It's difficult to bond with since it won't listen to its Trainer.

pokemon.name: Prinplup
pokemon.description: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group. It searches for prey in icy seas.

pokemon.name: Empoleon
pokemon.description: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. It avoids unnecessary disputes, but it will decimate anything that threatens its pride.

pokemon.name: Starly
pokemon.description: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. However, they bicker if the group grows too big. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

pokemon.name: Staravia
pokemon.description: Recognizing their own weakness, they maintain huge flocks. Fierce scuffles break out between various flocks. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

pokemon.name: Staraptor
pokemon.description: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. It has a savage nature. It will courageously challenge foes that are much larger.

pokemon.name: Mega-Staraptor
pokemon.description: Mega Staraptor is a top-class flier. It can easily soar through the sky while gripping a Steelix that weighs more than 880 lbs.

pokemon.name: Bidoof
pokemon.description: A comparison revealed that Bidoof's front teeth grow at the same rate as Rattata's. It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth.

pokemon.name: Bibarel
pokemon.description: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. A river dammed by Bibarel will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by people nearby.

pokemon.name: Kricketot
pokemon.description: It shakes its head back to front, causing its antennae to hit each other and sound like a xylophone. These sounds are fall hallmarks.

pokemon.name: Kricketune
pokemon.description: It signals its emotions with its melodies. There is a village that hosts a contest based on the amazingly variable cries of this Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Shinx
pokemon.description: It rapidly contracts and relaxes its muscles to generate electricity. Its body shines if endangered. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

pokemon.name: Luxio
pokemon.description: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch has enough amperage to cause fainting in foes.

pokemon.name: Luxray
pokemon.description: It has eyes which can see through anything. Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

pokemon.name: Cranidos
pokemon.description: A lifelong jungle dweller from 100 million years ago, its skull is as hard as iron. It would snap obstructing trees with headbutts.

pokemon.name: Rampardos
pokemon.description: The result of repeated headbutts is a skull grown thick and hard. However, its brain has shrunk in size compared with Cranidos's.

pokemon.name: Shieldon
pokemon.description: This Pokémon lived in primeval jungles. Few enemies would have been willing to square off against its heavily armored face, so it's thought.

pokemon.name: Bastiodon
pokemon.description: Their rock-hard faces serve to protect them from any frontral attacks. When attacked, they form a wall. They shielded their young in that way.

pokemon.name: Burmy (Plant)
pokemon.description: If its cloak is even slightly damaged, it will immediately repair it with whatever is near at hand. The Pokémon within the cloak is scrawny and vulnerable to the cold.

pokemon.name: Burmy (Sandy)
pokemon.description: It weaves a cloak together with sand, mud, and silk it spits out. This earthen cloak is ruined by wind and rain, so the Pokémon hides away in caves and other such places.

pokemon.name: Burmy (Trash)
pokemon.description: When confronted by a lack of other materials, Burmy will create its cloak using dust and refuse. The cloak seems to be more comfortable than one would think.

pokemon.name: Wormadam (Plant)
pokemon.description: Its appearance changes depending on where Burmy evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body. The cloak is never shed.

pokemon.name: Wormadam (Sandy)
pokemon.description: Its earthen skin is reasonably hard, it has no problem repelling a Starly's pecking. It is said that a Wormadam that evolves on a cold day will have a thicker cloak.

pokemon.name: Wormadam (Trash)
pokemon.description: Its body, composed of refuse, blends in to the scenery enough to be inconspicuous. This seems to be the perfect way for it to evade the detection of predators.

pokemon.name: Mothim (Plant)
pokemon.description: It does not keep a nest. While it loves floral honey, it won't gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal honey collected by Combee.

pokemon.name: Mothim (Sandy)
pokemon.description: It does not keep a nest. While it loves floral honey, it won't gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal honey collected by Combee.

pokemon.name: Mothim (Trash)
pokemon.description: It does not keep a nest. While it loves floral honey, it won't gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal honey collected by Combee.

pokemon.name: Combee
pokemon.description: A Pokémon formed by three others. It constantly gathers honey from flowers to please Vespiquen. At night, they cluster to form a beehive and sleep.

pokemon.name: Vespiquen
pokemon.description: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding. There is only one in a colony.

pokemon.name: Pachirisu
pokemon.description: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

pokemon.name: Buizel
pokemon.description: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement. It swims by rotating its two tails like a screw.

pokemon.name: Floatzel
pokemon.description: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

pokemon.name: Cherubi
pokemon.description: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is also very sweet and tasty. Pokémon like Starly and Taillow try to peck it off.

pokemon.name: Cherrim (Overcast)
pokemon.description: It's motionless, save for the occasional quiver. A rich array of Pokémon can be found gathered around it, drawn by the scent exuded from Cherrim's folded petals.

pokemon.name: Cherrim (Sunshine)
pokemon.description: Cherrim takes this form on sunny days. Its faint scent entices bug Pokémon to it. It immediately closes its petals and becomes immobile once the sun hides.

pokemon.name: Shellos (West Sea)
pokemon.description: It oozes a purple fluid to deter enemies. While harmless, the fluid is awfully sticky. Apparently, there are more West Sea Shellos now than there were in the past.

pokemon.name: Shellos (East Sea)
pokemon.description: It's capable of spending a limited amount of time on land until their skin dries out. One theory suggests that living in cold seas causes Shellos to take on this form.

pokemon.name: Gastrodon (West Sea)
pokemon.description: It appears on shallow-water beaches to eat sand for nourishment. Should one Gastrodon encounter another of a different color, a fierce battle will ensue.

pokemon.name: Gastrodon (East Sea)
pokemon.description: Found more often on land than in the sea. They normally live in rocky seashores, but when cloudy or rainy, they can sometimes be found on mountains, far from the sea.

pokemon.name: Drifloon
pokemon.description: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a “Signpost for Wandering Spirits.” Children holding them sometimes vanish.

pokemon.name: Drifblim
pokemon.description: Even while under careful observation, large flocks of Drifblim flying at dusk will inexplicably disappear from view. No one knows where they go.

pokemon.name: Buneary
pokemon.description: You can tell how it feels by the way it rolls its ears. When it's scared, both ears are rolled up. When it senses danger, it perks them up.

pokemon.name: Lopunny
pokemon.description: An extremely cautious Pokémon. It is very conscious of its looks and never fails to groom its ears. It runs with sprightly jumps.

pokemon.name: Mega-Lopunny
pokemon.description: Mega Evolution awakens its combative instincts. It has shed any fur that got in the way of its attacks. crescent.

pokemon.name: Glameow
pokemon.description: It is a very fickle Pokémon, which is very popular among some. It claws its Trainer's nose if it isn't fed and purrs when affectionate.

pokemon.name: Purugly
pokemon.description: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon's nest and claims it as its own. It binds its body with its tails to make itself look bigger.

pokemon.name: Stunky
pokemon.description: It protects itself by spraying a nose-curling, stinky fluid from its rear to repel attackers. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

pokemon.name: Skuntank
pokemon.description: It attacks by spraying a horribly smelly fluid from the tip of its tail. The fluid smells worse the longer it is allowed to fester.

pokemon.name: Bronzor
pokemon.description: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. There are researchers who believe this Pokémon reflected like a mirror in the distant past.

pokemon.name: Bronzong
pokemon.description: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. One caused a news stir when it was dug up at a construction site after a 2000-year sleep.

pokemon.name: Chatot
pokemon.description: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it's one of them. This way they won't attack it. It can also learn and speak human words.

pokemon.name: Spiritomb
pokemon.description: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

pokemon.name: Gible
pokemon.description: It nests in horizontal holes warmed by geothermal heat. Foes who stray too close can expect to be pounced on and bitten.

pokemon.name: Gabite
pokemon.description: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it's amassed or Carbink it has caught.

pokemon.name: Garchomp
pokemon.description: When it folds up its body and extends its wings, it looks like a jet plane. It is covered in fine scales that reduce drag, enabling it to fly at high speeds.

pokemon.name: Mega-Garchomp
pokemon.description: Excess energy melted its arms and wings into giant scythes, sending it mad with rage. It swings its scythes wildly and slices the ground to pieces.

pokemon.name: Mega-Garchomp-Z
pokemon.description: Garchomp has gained a new Mega-Evolved form. It flies around foes at Mach speed and cuts them to shreds with its sinister wing claws.

pokemon.name: Riolu
pokemon.description: It can discern the physical and emotional states of people, Pokémon, and other natural things from the shape of their aura waves.

pokemon.name: Lucario
pokemon.description: It understands human speech. It is said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect Auras. Even foes it could not otherwise see.

pokemon.name: Mega-Lucario
pokemon.description: It readies itself to face its enemies by focusing its mental energies. Its fighting style can be summed up in a single word: heartless.

pokemon.name: Mega-Lucario-Z
pokemon.description: By completely cloaking itself in its aura, Mega Lucario Z can parry all manner of attacks, battling as if it were gracefully dancing.

pokemon.name: Hippopotas
pokemon.description: Hippopotas shuts its nostrils tight when travelling through sand. Instead of perspiration, it expels grains of sand from its body.

pokemon.name: Hippowdon
pokemon.description: It is surprisingly quick to anger. It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength and raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.

pokemon.name: Skorupi
pokemon.description: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes. It can survive a year without food.

pokemon.name: Drapion
pokemon.description: Possessing a sturdy build, it takes pride in its strength. It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap out of a car.

pokemon.name: Croagunk
pokemon.description: Croagunk rarely fights fairly. Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

pokemon.name: Toxicroak
pokemon.description: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.

pokemon.name: Carnivine
pokemon.description: Hanging from branches using its tentacles, it looks like a plant. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down.

pokemon.name: Finneon
pokemon.description: Its double tail fins propel its energetic jumps. When it breaks the surface of the sea, Wingull swoop down to grab it on the fly.

pokemon.name: Lumineon
pokemon.description: Lumineon swimming in the darkness of the deep sea look like stars shining in the night sky. It competes for food with Lanturn.

pokemon.name: Snover
pokemon.description: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. Seemingly curious about people, they gather around footsteps they find on snowy mountains.

pokemon.name: Abomasnow
pokemon.description: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards. It is also known as “The Ice Monster.”

pokemon.name: Mega-Abomasnow
pokemon.description: The sprouts on Abomasnow's back grow into great shafts of ice when it Mega Evolves. Dislikes associating with others and chooses to live quietly deep in mountains.

pokemon.name: Rotom (Normal)
pokemon.description: Its body is composed of plasma and loves to surprise others. One boy's invention led to the development of many machines that can use of Rotom's unique capabilities.

pokemon.name: Rotom (Heat)
pokemon.description: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic. It makes mischief by turning up the heat.

pokemon.name: Rotom (Wash)
pokemon.description: It enjoys coming up with water-based pranks. The model of washing machine that Rotom can inspirit has been discontinued, so they are now traded at high prices.

pokemon.name: Rotom (Frost)
pokemon.description: In this form, Rotom likes to play freezing cold pranks. You may find it's turned the bath you just filled to solid ice! It battles by spewing cold air.

pokemon.name: Rotom (Fan)
pokemon.description: The first appliance developed that Rotom coud inspirit was the electric fan. It uses its power over wind in its pranks, happily blowing any important documents it finds.

pokemon.name: Rotom (Mow)
pokemon.description: The lawn mower is an appliance that led to the development of the Rotom Dex. It will mow down grass and flowers, then swagger around with pride at its accomplishments.

pokemon.name: Uxie
pokemon.description: Known as ”The Being of Knowledge,” according to some sources, this Pokémon provided people with the intelligence necessary to solve various problems.

pokemon.name: Mesprit
pokemon.description: Although it slumbers at the bottom of a lake, its spirit is said to leave its body and flitter on the water surface. It taught humans of sorrow, pain, and joy.

pokemon.name: Azelf
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is said to have endowed humans with the determination needed to do things. It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit and Azelf all came from the same egg.

pokemon.name: Dialga
pokemon.description: A Pokémon spoken of in legend. It completely controls the flow of time. It uses its power to travel at will through the past and future.

pokemon.name: Dialga (Origin)
pokemon.description: Radiant light caused Dialga to take on a form bearing a striking resemblance to the creator Pokémon. It wields such a colossal strength that this might be its true form.

pokemon.name: Palkia
pokemon.description: Palkia has the ability to distort space. Its total control over the boundaries of space enable it to transport itself to faraway places and other dimensions.

pokemon.name: Palkia (Origin)
pokemon.description: It soars across the sky in a form that resembles the creator of all things. Perhaps this imitation of appearance is a strategy for gaining Arceus's powers.

pokemon.name: Heatran
pokemon.description: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It dwells in volcanic caves, using its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

pokemon.name: Mega-Heatran
pokemon.description: It's said that if it goes all out, it can heat its body up to temperatures over 1.8 million degrees Fahrenheit. This heat keeps enemies at bay.

pokemon.name: Regigigas
pokemon.description: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon shaped Regirock, Regice, and Registeel out of clay, ice, and magma.

pokemon.name: Giratina (Altered)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange. It was banished for its violence.

pokemon.name: Giratina (Origin)
pokemon.description: Giratina loses its legs upon changing into this form. It's believed it hails from a world where the heavens and the earth are as one.

pokemon.name: Cresselia
pokemon.description: Those who sleep holding one of Cresselia's feathers are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

pokemon.name: Phione
pokemon.description: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs. It always returns to where it was born.

pokemon.name: Manaphy
pokemon.description: Water makes up 80% of its body. It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Darkrai
pokemon.description: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers. However, it means no harm.

pokemon.name: Mega-Darkrai
pokemon.description: Its dark power blocks out the sun, plunging the surrounding area into darkness. There is no escaping its evil eye.

pokemon.name: Shaymin (Land)
pokemon.description: The flowers all over its body bloom if it is lovingly hugged and senses gratitude. It dissolves toxins in the air to transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

pokemon.name: Shaymin (Sky)
pokemon.description: Upon taking in the scent of a particular rare flower, Shaymin is enveloped in light and its tiny body transforms, confering the power of flight upon it.

pokemon.name: Arceus (Normal)
pokemon.description: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world, before the universe even existed.

pokemon.name: Arceus (Fighting)
pokemon.description: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world, before the universe even existed.
