pokemon.name: Arceus (Fairy)
pokemon.description: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world, before the universe even existed.

pokemon.name: Victini
pokemon.description: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

pokemon.name: Snivy
pokemon.description: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

pokemon.name: Servine
pokemon.description: When it gets dirty, its leaves can't be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps clean. They avoid attacks by sinking into the shadows of thick foliage.

pokemon.name: Serperior
pokemon.description: They raise their heads to intimidate foes, but only give it their all when fighting powerful opponents who are not fazed by the glare from its noble eyes.

pokemon.name: Tepig
pokemon.description: It loves to eat roasted berries, but sometimes it gets too excited and burns them to a crisp. It blows fire through its nose.

pokemon.name: Pignite
pokemon.description: The more it eats, the more fuel it has to make the fire in its stomach stronger. When it is angered, the intensity of the flame increases.

pokemon.name: Emboar
pokemon.description: It is adept at using many different moves. It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

pokemon.name: Mega-Emboar
pokemon.description: Brandishing a blazing flame shaped like a serpentine spear, it rushes in to save its imperiled allies.

pokemon.name: Oshawott
pokemon.description: The scalchop on its stomach isn't just used for battle, it can be used to break open hard berries as well. It is made from the same element as its claws.

pokemon.name: Dewott
pokemon.description: Strict training and disclipine leads it to master its flowing double-scalchop technique. Scalchop techniques differ from one Dewott to another.

pokemon.name: Samurott
pokemon.description: One swing of the seamitars attached to its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them is enough to intimidate most enemies.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Samurott
pokemon.description: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui.

pokemon.name: Patrat
pokemon.description: Extremely cautious, they take shifts to maintain a constant watch of their nest. Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days.

pokemon.name: Watchog
pokemon.description: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents. Keen eyesight lets them see in the dark.

pokemon.name: Lillipup
pokemon.description: It faces strong opponents with great courage. Because it doesn't yelp, it's extremely popular with Trainers who live in apartment buildings.

pokemon.name: Herdier
pokemon.description: Its dense black fur grows continuously. The high cost of keeping its hard fur properly groomed makes this a troublesome Pokémon to train.

pokemon.name: Stoutland
pokemon.description: Intelligent, good-natured, and valiant, there could be no concern that it would ever attack people. Some parents even trust it to babysit.

pokemon.name: Purrloin
pokemon.description: Their deceptively cute act is the perfect ruse. They steal from people for fun, but their victims can't help but forgive them.

pokemon.name: Liepard
pokemon.description: Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur. These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. They run silently in the night.

pokemon.name: Pansage
pokemon.description: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. It's good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It's kind enough to share them with friends.

pokemon.name: Simisage
pokemon.description: Ill tempered, it attacks enemies by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

pokemon.name: Pansear
pokemon.description: The fire within the tuft on its head can reach up to 600ºF. Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

pokemon.name: Simisear
pokemon.description: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

pokemon.name: Panpour
pokemon.description: The water stored inside the tuft of its head is rich in nutrients. It waters plants using its tail, plants that receive its water grow large.

pokemon.name: Simipour
pokemon.description: It can shoot highly-pressurised water from its tail with enough power to break through a solid concrete wall. It prefers places with clean water.

pokemon.name: Munna
pokemon.description: This Pokémon appears before people and Pokémon who are having nightmares and eats those dreams. Munna always float in the air.

pokemon.name: Musharna
pokemon.description: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon. It changes into different colors depending on the dream that was eaten.

pokemon.name: Pidove
pokemon.description: This very forgetful Pokémon will wait for a new order from its Trainer even though it already has one. Flocks often gather in parks and plazas.

pokemon.name: Tranquill
pokemon.description: No matter where in the world it goes, it knows where its nest is, so it can always return to its Trainer's location regardless of the distance.

pokemon.name: Unfezant
pokemon.description: Males have plumage on their heads. The females' flying abilities surpass those of the males. They will only let themselves feel close to their Trainers.

pokemon.name: Blitzle
pokemon.description: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate with one another.

pokemon.name: Zebstrika
pokemon.description: When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates. It is dangerous when it's angry as it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

pokemon.name: Roggenrola
pokemon.description: The hexagonal cavity is its ear. It walks in the direction of sounds it hears, but if the sounds cease, it panics and topples over.

pokemon.name: Boldore
pokemon.description: Its orange crystal is a mass of energy. Just one crystal fragment would provide enough fuel for a hundred dump trucks. When it is healthy, its core sticks out.

pokemon.name: Gigalith
pokemon.description: The blasts of energy it makes from sunbeams have terrifying power. However, it's not able to fire its blasts at night or on rainy days.

pokemon.name: Woobat
pokemon.description: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. Suction from its nostrils enables it to stick to cave walls during sleep. It leaves a heart-shaped mark behind.

pokemon.name: Swoobat
pokemon.description: Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift.

pokemon.name: Drilbur
pokemon.description: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph by spinning its body. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

pokemon.name: Excadrill
pokemon.description: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels, but it can also help with construction.

pokemon.name: Mega-Excadrill
pokemon.description: If this Pokémon brings its arms and head together to form a streamlined shape and spins at high speeds, it can destroy anything.

pokemon.name: Audino
pokemon.description: Its auditory sense is astounding. Using the feelers on its ears, it can tell how someone is feeling or when an egg might hatch.

pokemon.name: Mega-Audino
pokemon.description: Mega Audino emits a soothing pulse from its body that reduces hostility in others. Anything that comes into contact with its second feelers will fall into a deep sleep.

pokemon.name: Timburr
pokemon.description: These Pokémon appear at building sites and help out with construction. They always carry squared logs. As they grow, they carry bigger logs.

pokemon.name: Gurdurr
pokemon.description: They strengthen their bodies by carrying steel beams. This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

pokemon.name: Conkeldurr
pokemon.description: They use concrete pillars as walking canes. Rather than rely solely on force, they master moves that utilize the centrifugal force of spinning concrete.

pokemon.name: Tympole
pokemon.description: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk and warns others of danger.

pokemon.name: Palpitoad
pokemon.description: It lives in the water and on land. When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

pokemon.name: Seismitoad
pokemon.description: It increases the power of its punches by vibrating the bumps on its fists. They can also shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps.

pokemon.name: Throh
pokemon.description: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts. It changes belts as it gets more powerful.

pokemon.name: Sawk
pokemon.description: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train. Disturbing their training angers them.

pokemon.name: Sewaddle
pokemon.description: It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth. Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes, it is a mascot for fashion designers.

pokemon.name: Swadloon
pokemon.description: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

pokemon.name: Leavanny
pokemon.description: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to weave warm wrappings for Sewaddle by using the silk secreted from its mouth.

pokemon.name: Venipede
pokemon.description: Using the feelers on its head and tail, it picks up vibrations in the air to determine its prey's location and state. It is brutally aggressive.

pokemon.name: Whirlipede
pokemon.description: Storing energy for evolution, it sits. But when attacked, it spins its body at high speed like a wheel and then crashes furiously into its opponent.

pokemon.name: Scolipede
pokemon.description: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails. Then it finishes them off with deadly poison.

pokemon.name: Mega-Scolipede
pokemon.description: Its deadly venom gives off a faint glow. The venom affects Scolipede's mind, honing its viciousness.

pokemon.name: Cottonee
pokemon.description: When it finds others of its kind, they all stick together. When enough of them have collected, the mass resembles a cumulonimbus cloud.

pokemon.name: Whimsicott
pokemon.description: It rides on the wind and slips into people's homes. After it has turned a room into a cotton-filled mess, it giggles to itself and takes off.

pokemon.name: Petilil
pokemon.description: By pruning the leaves on its head with regularity, this Pokémon can be grown into a fine plump shape. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

pokemon.name: Lilligant
pokemon.description: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. It withers away if a trainer does not take good care of it.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Lilligant
pokemon.description: Its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

pokemon.name: Basculin (Red)
pokemon.description: It used to be a common food source. The meat of red Basculin is on the fatty side and it's more popular with the youth. Its temperament is vicious and aggressive.

pokemon.name: Basculin (Blue)
pokemon.description: They form schools with others of the same color. The greatest foe of a school of blue Basculin is a school of red Basculin. Its flavor is light and inoffensive.

pokemon.name: Basculin (White)
pokemon.description: While this ancient form of Basculin shares a vast array of qualities with other forms, it differs in several respects, including demeanor, being much gentler than others.

pokemon.name: Basculegion (♂)
pokemon.description: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species in all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

pokemon.name: Basculegion (♀)
pokemon.description: The souls of its comrades cloak its body and propel it effortlessly through even raging rapids.

pokemon.name: Sandile
pokemon.description: It conceals itself in the sand and chomps down on the legs of any prey that unwarily walk over it. Its favorite food is Trapinch.

pokemon.name: Krokorok
pokemon.description: They move in groups of a few individuals. Thanks to the special membrane covering its eyes, it can see its surroundings clearly, even in the dead of night.

pokemon.name: Krookodile
pokemon.description: Very violent Pokémon, they never allow their prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

pokemon.name: Darumaka
pokemon.description: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it pulls its limbs into its body and falls asleep.

pokemon.name: Darmanitan
pokemon.description: When one is injured in a fierce battle, it transforms into a stone statue. Then it meditates and sharpens its mind and fights on mentally.

pokemon.name: Darmanitan (Zen)
pokemon.description: When wounded, it stops moving. It goes as still as stone to meditate, sharpening its mind and spirit.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Darumaka
pokemon.description: The colder they get, the more energetic they are. They freeze their breath to make snowballs, using them as ammo for playful snowball fights.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Darmanitan
pokemon.description: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Darmanitan (Zen)
pokemon.description: Darmanitan takes this form when enraged. It won't stop spewing flames until its rage has settled, even if its body starts to melt.

pokemon.name: Maractus
pokemon.description: Arid regions are their habitat. When it moves rhythmically, it makes a sound similar to maracas, making the surprised Pokémon flee.

pokemon.name: Dwebble
pokemon.description: It makes a hole in a stone of a suitable size. If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement.

pokemon.name: Crustle
pokemon.description: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle and begins to weaken.

pokemon.name: Scraggy
pokemon.description: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck. Its skull is massively thick.

pokemon.name: Scrafty
pokemon.description: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. The one with the biggest crest is the group leader, and is the most respected.

pokemon.name: Mega-Scrafty
pokemon.description: Mega Evolution has caused Scrafty's shed skin to turn white, growing tough and supple. Of course, this Pokémon is still as feisty as ever.

pokemon.name: Sigilyph
pokemon.description: The guardians of an ancient city, they always fly the same route while keeping watch for invaders. They use their psychic power to attack enemies.

pokemon.name: Yamask
pokemon.description: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves in past ages. Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human.

pokemon.name: Cofagrigus
pokemon.description: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies. Their bodies are covered in pure gold.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Yamask
pokemon.description: A clay slab with cursed engravings took possession of a Yamask. The slab is said to be absorbing the Yamask's dark power.

pokemon.name: Runerigus
pokemon.description: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

pokemon.name: Tirtouga
pokemon.description: Reputed to be the ancestor of most turtle Pokémon, it lived in warm seas about a hundred million years ago. It was restored from a fossil.

pokemon.name: Carracosta
pokemon.description: They can live both in the ocean and on land. Incredible jaw strength enables them to chew up steel beams and rocks along with their prey.

pokemon.name: Archen
pokemon.description: Said to be the ancestor of all bird Pokémon, Archen itself could not actually fly but moved by hopping from treetop to treetop.

pokemon.name: Archeops
pokemon.description: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. When one Archeops had the prey cornered, another would swoop on it.

pokemon.name: Trubbish
pokemon.description: It gorges on trash until its stomach is full. Then it belches toxic gas. An unlucky whiff of this gas will put a person in the hospital.

pokemon.name: Garbodor
pokemon.description: Beware the poisonous liquid it shoots from its right arm. If even a little of it gets on you, you'll experience the full effects of this unidentified toxin.

pokemon.name: GMax-Garbodor
pokemon.description: It sprays toxic gas from its mouth and fingers. If the gas engulfs you, the toxins will seep in all the way down to your bones.

pokemon.name: Zorua
pokemon.description: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

pokemon.name: Zoroark
pokemon.description: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents. They defend their lair with illusory scenery.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Zorua
pokemon.description: Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Zoroark
pokemon.description: Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.

pokemon.name: Minccino
pokemon.description: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

pokemon.name: Cinccino
pokemon.description: Their white fur feels amazing to touch. Cinccino's body is coated in a special oil that repels dust and helps it deflect attacks, such as punches.

pokemon.name: Gothita
pokemon.description: They intently observe both Trainers and Pokémon. Apparently, Gothita are looking at something that only they can see. Their ribbonlike feelers increase their power.

pokemon.name: Gothorita
pokemon.description: Starlight is the source of their power. They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading them astray are told in every corner.

pokemon.name: Gothitelle
pokemon.description: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power. When it learns its Trainer's life span, it cries in sadness.

pokemon.name: Solosis
pokemon.description: Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they are fine in any environment, no matter how severe. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

pokemon.name: Duosion
pokemon.description: When their brains, now divided in two, are thinking the same thoughts, these Pokémon exhibit their maximized psychic power.

pokemon.name: Reuniclus
pokemon.description: They use psychic power to control their arms, which are made of a special liquid. When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains.

pokemon.name: Ducklett
pokemon.description: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

pokemon.name: Swanna
pokemon.description: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles. The one in the middle is the leader of the flock.

pokemon.name: Vanillite
pokemon.description: The temperature of their breath is -58ºF. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them. Vanillite is treasured by households in warm regions.

pokemon.name: Vanillish
pokemon.description: Vanillish has existed since the Ice Age. It controls particles of ice, freezes its opponents, and then shatters them with a headbutt.

pokemon.name: Vanilluxe
pokemon.description: Each of its two heads has a brain, and when they are in agreement, it attacks its enemies by exhaling a violent blizzard. It makes snow clouds inside its body.

pokemon.name: Deerling (Spring)
pokemon.description: Deerling have different scents depending on the season. In early spring, these Pokémon give off a delicate, sweet, and calming scent.

pokemon.name: Deerling (Summer)
pokemon.description: To prevent Deerling from entering their fields, many farmers will have several Lycanroc stand guard, as they are its natural enemy.

pokemon.name: Deerling (Autumn)
pokemon.description: Deerling have different scents depending on the season. In this form, Deerling have a softly sweet scent that lingers in the nose.

pokemon.name: Deerling (Winter)
pokemon.description: Deerling's scent changes with the seasons, but when the Pokémon is in its Winter Form, it has hardly any scent at all.

pokemon.name: Sawsbuck (Spring)
pokemon.description: Some people call Sawsbuck the harbingers of spring because the plants growing on its horns change according to the season. The leader possesses magnificent horns.

pokemon.name: Sawsbuck (Summer)
pokemon.description: The thicker and larger the branches and leaves on its antlers, the more majestic the Sawsbuck is thought to be.

pokemon.name: Sawsbuck (Autumn)
pokemon.description: Sawsbuck in their Autumn Form are known for having very short tempers. The males fight each other incessantly.

pokemon.name: Sawsbuck (Winter)
pokemon.description: Though there are many enthusiasts for Sawsbuck as a species, this seasonal form in particular is not very popular.

pokemon.name: Emolga
pokemon.description: It glides using its cape-like membrane. It grills berries and bug Pokémon with electric shocks from the pouches on its cheeks and makes a meal of them.

pokemon.name: Karrablast
pokemon.description: When they feel threatened, they spit an acidic liquid to drive attackers away. For some reason they evolve while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

pokemon.name: Escavalier
pokemon.description: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances. The steel armor protects their whole body.

pokemon.name: Foongus
pokemon.description: It lures Pokémon in with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball, then releases its poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

pokemon.name: Amoonguss
pokemon.description: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion, but very few Pokémon are fooled by this.

pokemon.name: Frillish
pokemon.description: If its veil-like arms stun and wrap an opponent, that foe will be dragged five miles below the surface to its lair, never to return.

pokemon.name: Jellicent
pokemon.description: They propel themselves by expelling absorbed seawater from their bodies. It's said there's a castle of ships Jellicent have sunk on the seafloor.

pokemon.name: Alomomola
pokemon.description: When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore. The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

pokemon.name: Joltik
pokemon.description: Since they can't generate their own electricity, they attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in a pouch.

pokemon.name: Galvantula
pokemon.description: It creates barriers from electrified silk that stun foes. This works as a weapon as well as a defense. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it.

pokemon.name: Ferroseed
pokemon.description: It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling of caves. When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes.

pokemon.name: Ferrothorn
pokemon.description: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers and shooting spikes. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

pokemon.name: Klink
pokemon.description: Two bodies comprise a fixed pair. The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

pokemon.name: Klang
pokemon.description: A minigear and big gear comprise its body. Spinning minigears are rotated at high speed and repeatedly fired away. It is dangerous if the gears don't return.

pokemon.name: Klinklang
pokemon.description: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It is rotated at high speed for a rapid energy charge. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

pokemon.name: Tynamo
pokemon.description: These Pokémon move in schools. One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

pokemon.name: Eelektrik
pokemon.description: These Pokémon have a big appetite. It wraps itself around its prey and paralyzes it with electricity from the round spots on its sides. Then it chomps.

pokemon.name: Eelektross
pokemon.description: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean, with their sucker mouths.

pokemon.name: Mega-Eelektross
pokemon.description: It now generates 10 times the electricity it did before Mega Evolving. It discharges this energy from its false Eelektrik made of mucus.

pokemon.name: Elgyem
pokemon.description: It psychically squeezes the brain of its foe, causing unendurable headaches. Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

pokemon.name: Beheeyem
pokemon.description: It can manipulate an opponent's memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three fingers, but those patterns haven't been decoded.

pokemon.name: Litwick
pokemon.description: While Litwick shines a light and pretends to be a guide, it absorbs the life force of any who follow it, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

pokemon.name: Lampent
pokemon.description: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen. The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire.

pokemon.name: Chandelure
pokemon.description: Being consumed in Chandelure's flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

pokemon.name: Mega-Chandelure
pokemon.description: One of its eyes is a window linking our world with the afterlife. This Pokémon draws in hatred and converts it into power.

pokemon.name: Axew
pokemon.description: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly, harder and sturdier than the last.

pokemon.name: Fraxure
pokemon.description: Their tusks can shatter rocks. A broken tusk will not grow back, so it diligently sharpens its tusks on river rocks after the end of a battle.

pokemon.name: Haxorus
pokemon.description: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams.

pokemon.name: Cubchoo
pokemon.description: Its nose is always running. Their snot is a barometer of health. When healthy, their snot is sticky and the power of their ice moves increases.

pokemon.name: Beartic
pokemon.description: It freezes its breath to create fangs and claws of ice to fight with. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

pokemon.name: Cryogonal
pokemon.description: They are born in snow clouds. When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

pokemon.name: Shelmet
pokemon.description: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It evolves when bathed in an electric-like energy along with Karrablast.

pokemon.name: Accelgor
pokemon.description: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in layers of thin membrane. It is very light and fights with ninja-like movements.

pokemon.name: Stunfisk
pokemon.description: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

pokemon.name: Galarian-Stunfisk
pokemon.description: Its conspicuous lips lure prey in as it lies in wait in the mud. When prey gets close, Stunfisk clamps its jagged steel fins down on them.

pokemon.name: Mienfoo
pokemon.description: They have mastered elegant combos. As they concentrate, their battle moves become swifter and more precise. What it lacks in power, it makes up for in quantity.

pokemon.name: Mienshao
pokemon.description: They use the long fur on their arms as a whip to strike their opponents. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

pokemon.name: Druddigon
pokemon.description: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move. It uses its sharp claws to catch prey.

pokemon.name: Golett
pokemon.description: These Pokémon are thought to have been created by the science of an ancient and mysterious civilization. It's been active for thousands of years.

pokemon.name: Golurk
pokemon.description: Golurk were created to protect people and Pokémon by an ancient civilisation. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

pokemon.name: Mega-Golurk
pokemon.description: The energy within Golurk has been stimulated by Mega Evolution. The Pokémon could explode at any moment.

pokemon.name: Pawniard
pokemon.description: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey. If battling dulls them, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

pokemon.name: Bisharp
pokemon.description: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

pokemon.name: Kingambit
pokemon.description: Though it commands a massive army in battle, it's not skilled at devising complex strategies. It just uses brute strength to keep pushing.

pokemon.name: Bouffalant
pokemon.description: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt. Their headbutts have enough destructive force to derail a train.

pokemon.name: Rufflet
pokemon.description: With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it's a carnivore at heart.

pokemon.name: Braviary
pokemon.description: For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if it is injured. One can carry a car while flying.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Braviary
pokemon.description: Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt.

pokemon.name: Vullaby
pokemon.description: It can't fly yet and must wait until its wings have developed more. They guard their posteriors with bones that were gathered by Mandibuzz.

pokemon.name: Mandibuzz
pokemon.description: They adorn themselves beautifully with bones. This is supposedly an effort to attract males, but no male Mandibuzz have ever been found.

pokemon.name: Heatmor
pokemon.description: It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. They burn through Durant's steel bodies and consume their insides.

pokemon.name: Durant
pokemon.description: Durant dig nests in mountains. Individuals each play different roles in driving Heatmor, their natural predator, away from their colony.

pokemon.name: Deino
pokemon.description: Lacking sight, it's unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

pokemon.name: Zweilous
pokemon.description: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves on to another area. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

pokemon.name: Hydreigon
pokemon.description: This brutal Pokémon travels the skies on its six wings. The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

pokemon.name: Larvesta
pokemon.description: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. It spews fire from its horns and encases itself in a cocoon of fire when it evolves.

pokemon.name: Volcarona
pokemon.description: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it appeared and saved Pokémon from freezing, Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

pokemon.name: Cobalion
pokemon.description: It has a body and heart of steel. This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

pokemon.name: Terrakion
pokemon.description: Spoken of in legend, this Pokémon used its phenomenal power to destroy a castle in its effort to protect Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

pokemon.name: Virizion
pokemon.description: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Legends say this Pokémon used whirlwind-like movements to confound and swiftly cut its opponents.

pokemon.name: Tornadus (Incarnate)
pokemon.description: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power can blow entire houses away. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

pokemon.name: Tornadus (Therian)
pokemon.description: An example of what is known as a “form change”. Tornadus has been sighted crossing the ocean while in this form.

pokemon.name: Thundurus (Incarnate)
pokemon.description: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed.

pokemon.name: Thundurus (Therian)
pokemon.description: It pulverizes foes into nothingness with showers of devastatingly powerful lightning bolts launched from the string of orbs on its tail.

pokemon.name: Reshiram
pokemon.description: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

pokemon.name: Zekrom
pokemon.description: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with lightning. It has a generator that creates electricity in its tail. It assists those with strong ideals.

pokemon.name: Landorus (Incarnate)
pokemon.description: The energy that comes pouring from its tail increases the nutrition in the soil, granting bountiful crops. It has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

pokemon.name: Landorus (Therian)
pokemon.description: Landorus soars through the sky in this form, bestowing plentiful harvests upon the land and earning the people's reverence.

pokemon.name: Kyurem
pokemon.description: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals. Its body became frozen when its energy leaked out.

pokemon.name: Kyurem-White
pokemon.description: The sameness of Reshiram's and Kyurem's genes allowed Kyurem to absorb Reshiram. Kyurem can now use the power of both fire and ice.

pokemon.name: Kyurem-Black
pokemon.description: The sameness of Zekrom's and Kyurem's genes allowed Kyurem to absorb Zekrom. Kyurem can now use the power of both electricity and ice.

pokemon.name: Keldeo (Ordianry)
pokemon.description: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. It crosses the world, running over the surfaces of oceans and rivers.

pokemon.name: Keldeo (Resolute)
pokemon.description: The power that lay hidden in its body now covers its horn, turning it into a sword that can slice through anything.

pokemon.name: Meloetta (Aria)
pokemon.description: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad. Many famous songs have been inspired by the tunes that Meloetta plays.

pokemon.name: Meloetta (Pirouette)
pokemon.description: Meloetta's melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

pokemon.name: Genesect (Normal)
pokemon.description: Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

pokemon.name: Genesect (Douse)
pokemon.description: Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

pokemon.name: Genesect (Shock)
pokemon.description: Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

pokemon.name: Genesect (Burn)
pokemon.description: Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

pokemon.name: Genesect (Chill)
pokemon.description: Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

pokemon.name: Chespin
pokemon.description: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock without any effort.

pokemon.name: Quilladin
pokemon.description: They strengthen the sturdy shell covering their bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won't start fights, but will counterattack with sharp quills.

pokemon.name: Chesnaught
pokemon.description: It shields its allies from danger with its own body. When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it can withstand a bomb blast.

pokemon.name: Mega-Chesnaught
pokemon.description: It has fortified armor and a will to defend at all costs. Both are absurdly strong.

pokemon.name: Fennekin
pokemon.description: As it walks, it munches on a twig to fill itself with energy in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its roomy ears.

pokemon.name: Braixen
pokemon.description: Braixen has a twig stuck in its tail. When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

pokemon.name: Delphox
pokemon.description: It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future. It uses psychic power to incinerate its foes.

pokemon.name: Mega-Delphox
pokemon.description: It wields flaming branches to dazzle its opponents before incinerating them with a huge fireball.

pokemon.name: Froakie
pokemon.description: It protects its skin by covering its body in bubbles it secretes from its chest and back. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

pokemon.name: Frogadier
pokemon.description: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away. Frogadier's swiftness is unparalleled.

pokemon.name: Greninja (Normal)
pokemon.description: It appears and vanishes with a ninja's grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars made of compressed water.

pokemon.name: Greninja (Battle Bond)
pokemon.description: It appears and vanishes with a ninja's grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars made of compressed water.

pokemon.name: Greninja (Ash)
pokemon.description: It appears and vanishes with a ninja's grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars made of compressed water.

pokemon.name: Mega-Greninja
pokemon.description: This Pokémon spins a giant shuriken at high speed to make it float, then clings to it upside down to catch opponents unawares.

pokemon.name: Bunnelby
pokemon.description: It has ears like shovels. Digging holes strengthens its ears so much that they can sever thick roots effortlessly. Bunnelby dig the whole night through.

pokemon.name: Diggersby
pokemon.description: With its powerful ears it reduces dense bedrock to rubble. It can be a big help at construction sites. When it's finished digging, it lounges about lazily.

pokemon.name: Fletchling
pokemon.description: This amiable Pokémon is easy to train. But when battle is joined, it shows its ferocious side. It's merciless to intruders that enter its territory.

pokemon.name: Fletchinder
pokemon.description: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on any bewildered Pokémon that pop out of the grass.

pokemon.name: Talonflame
pokemon.description: In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers and takes to the air. It finishes its prey off with a colossal kick.

pokemon.name: Scatterbug (Icy Snow)
pokemon.description: When under attack from bird Pokémon, it spews a poisonous black powder that causes paralysis on contact. Scatterbug can live in any region or climate.

pokemon.name: Spewpa (Icy Snow)
pokemon.description: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it quickly bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Icy Snow)
pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from frigid lands. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Polar)
pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from snowy lands. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Tundra)

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Continental)

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Garden)

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Meadow)

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Marine)

pokemon.name: Vivillon (High Plains)

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Sandstorm)

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Savanna)
pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from lands with a tropical climate. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Sun)
pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from lands bathed in light. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Ocean)
pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from lands of perpetual summer. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.name: Vivillon (Jungle)
pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from lands of tropical rainforests. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from a mysterious land. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.description: Its pattern depends on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from a special land. It scatters toxic color scales in battle.

pokemon.name: Litleo
pokemon.description: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to start a fight.

pokemon.name: Pyroar
pokemon.description: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride. The females protect the pride's cubs. They viciously threaten any challenger.

pokemon.name: Mega-Pyroar
pokemon.description: This Pokémon spews flames hotter than 18,000 degrees Fahrenheit. It swings around its grand, blazing mane as it protects its allies.

pokemon.name: Mega-Floette (Eternal)
pokemon.description: The Eternal Flower has absorbed all the energy from Mega Evolution. The flower now attacks enemies on its own.

pokemon.name: Skiddo
pokemon.description: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back. It has a placid disposition.

pokemon.name: Gogoat
pokemon.description: It can tell how its trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathetic sense lets them run as if one being. They inhabit mountainous regions.

pokemon.name: Pancham
pokemon.description: There's no point to the leaf in its mouth, aside from an effort to look cool. It's mischievous, so it's not well suited to inexperienced Trainers.

pokemon.name: Pangoro
pokemon.description: It boasts superb physical strength. Those who wish to become Pangoro's Trainer have no choice but to converse with their fists.

pokemon.name: Furfrou (Natural)
pokemon.description: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements. These Pokémon were the guardians of kings.

pokemon.name: Furfrou (Heart)
pokemon.description: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements. These Pokémon were the guardians of kings.

pokemon.name: Espurr
pokemon.description: It has enough psychic energy to blast everything within 300 feet of itself. The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears.

pokemon.name: Meowstic (♂)
pokemon.description: The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It's when they're protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power.

pokemon.name: Meowstic (♀)
pokemon.description: Females are a bit more selfish and aggressive than males. If they don't get what they want, they will torment you with their psychic abilities.

pokemon.name: Mega-Meowstic (♂)
pokemon.description: Mega Meowstic can use its psychic power to compress or expand anything. It overwhelms foes by contorting space itself.

pokemon.name: Mega-Meowstic (♀)
pokemon.description: Mega Meowstic can use its psychic power to compress or expand anything. It overwhelms foes by contorting space itself.

pokemon.name: Honedge
pokemon.description: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person's arm and drains that person's life energy completely.

pokemon.name: Doublade
pokemon.description: When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords. The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even against those skilled at swordplay.

pokemon.name: Aegislash (Shield)
pokemon.description: In this defensive stance, Aegislash uses its steel body and a force field of spectral power to reduce the damage of any attack.

pokemon.name: Aegislash (Blade)
pokemon.description: Once upon a time, a king with an Aegislash reigned over the land. His Pokémon eventually drained him of life, and his kingdom fell with him.

pokemon.name: Spritzee
pokemon.description: In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked. Its fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten.

pokemon.name: Aromatisse
pokemon.description: Its scent is so overpowering that, unless a Trainer happens to really enjoy the smell, he or she will have a hard time walking alongside it.

pokemon.name: Swirlix
pokemon.description: Because it eats nothing but sweets, its fur is as sticky sweet as cotton candy. To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes sticky white threads.

pokemon.name: Slurpuff
pokemon.description: Slurpuff can distinguish even the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

pokemon.name: Inkay
pokemon.description: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent's will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

pokemon.name: Malamar
pokemon.description: It lures prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids. It forces others to do whatever it wants.

pokemon.name: Mega-Malamar
pokemon.description: It uses its colorful lights to overwrite the personality and memories of others-and to control them.

pokemon.name: Binacle
pokemon.description: They stretch and then contract, yanking their rocks along with them in bold hops. They eat seaweed that washes up on the shoreline.

pokemon.name: Barbaracle
pokemon.description: Barbaracle's legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head's orders.

pokemon.name: Mega-Barbaracle
pokemon.description: It uses its many arms to toy with its opponents. This keeps the head extremely busy.

pokemon.name: Skrelp
pokemon.description: Camouflaged as rotten kelp, it hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution. They spray liquid poison on prey that approach unawares.

pokemon.name: Dragalge
pokemon.description: Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker. Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

pokemon.name: Mega-Dragalge
pokemon.description: It spits a liquid that causes the regenerative power of cells to run wild. The liquid is deadly poison to everything other than itself.

pokemon.name: Clauncher
pokemon.description: Through controlled expulsions of internal gas, it can expel water like a pistol shot. At close distances, it can even shatter large rocks.

pokemon.name: Clawitzer
pokemon.description: By expelling water from the nozzle in the back of its enormous claw, it can move at a speed of 60 knots. They launch cannonballs made of water.

pokemon.name: Helioptile
pokemon.description: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their own energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

pokemon.name: Heliolisk
pokemon.description: A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper. It can stimulate its muscles with electricity, boosting the strength in its legs.

pokemon.name: Tyrunt
pokemon.description: Its immense jaws have enough destructive force that it can chew up a car. If something happens that it doesn't like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

pokemon.name: Tyrantrum
pokemon.description: Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited.

pokemon.name: Amaura
pokemon.description: This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years. It lived in a cold land where there were no predators.

pokemon.name: Aurorus
pokemon.description: Using the diamond-shaped crystals on its body it can instantly create a wall of ice to block an opponent's attack, or encase them in ice.

pokemon.name: Hawlucha
pokemon.description: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. Its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama.

pokemon.name: Mega-Hawlucha
pokemon.description: Mega Evolution has pumped up all its muscles. Hawlucha flexes to show off its strength.

pokemon.name: Dedenne
pokemon.description: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

pokemon.name: Carbink
pokemon.description: Born from the high temperatures and pressures deep underground, it defends itself by firing beams from the jewel part of its body.

pokemon.name: Goomy
pokemon.description: Its source of protection is its slimy, germ-laden mucous membrane. Anyone who touches it will need some thorough hand-washing.

pokemon.name: Sliggoo
pokemon.description: This Pokémon's mucous can dissolve anything. Toothless, it sprays mucous on its prey. Once they're nicely dissolved, it slurps them up.

pokemon.name: Goodra
pokemon.description: It gets picked on because it's meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Sliggoo
pokemon.description: A creature given to melancholy. Its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Goodra
pokemon.description: It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy--it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

pokemon.name: Klefki
pokemon.description: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them. It will sneak into people's homes to steal their keys.

pokemon.name: Phantump
pokemon.description: According to legend, medicine to cure any illness can be made by plucking the green leaves on its head, brewing them, and boiling down the liquid.

pokemon.name: Trevenant
pokemon.description: Through its roots, it exerts control over other trees. A deadly curse falls upon anyone cutting down trees in forests where Trevenant dwell.

pokemon.name: Pumpkaboo (Average)
pokemon.description: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

pokemon.name: Pumpkaboo (Small)
pokemon.description: When taking spirits to the afterlife, small Pumpkaboo prefer the spirits of children to those of adults.

pokemon.name: Pumpkaboo (Large)
pokemon.description: When taking spirits to the afterlife, large Pumpkaboo prefer the spirits of adults to those of children.

pokemon.name: Pumpkaboo (Super)
pokemon.description: Supersized Pumpkaboo are very partial to the spirits of people who were of similarly superior proportions.

pokemon.name: Gourgeist (Average)
pokemon.description: Eerie cries emanate from its body in the dead of night. The sounds are said to be the wails of spirits who are suffering in the afterlife.

pokemon.name: Gourgeist (Small)
pokemon.description: A small-sized Pumpkaboo evolves into a small-sized Gourgeist. Its bodily proportions also get passed on to its descendants.

pokemon.name: Gourgeist (Large)
pokemon.description: A large-sized Pumpkaboo evolves into a large-sized Gourgeist. Its bodily proportions also get passed on to its descendants.

pokemon.name: Gourgeist (Super)
pokemon.description: A supersized Pumpkaboo evolves into a supersized Gourgeist. Its bodily proportions also get passed on to its descendants.

pokemon.name: Bergmite
pokemon.description: It blocks opponents' attacks with the ice that shields its body. It uses cold air to repair any cracks with new ice. They live in herds on snowy mountains.

pokemon.name: Avalugg
pokemon.description: The way several Bergmite huddle on its back make it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice. Its cumbersome frame crushes anything that stands in its way.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Avalugg
pokemon.description: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease.

pokemon.name: Noibat
pokemon.description: They live in pitch-black caves. Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

pokemon.name: Noivern
pokemon.description: They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. The ultrasonic waves it emits from its ears can reduce a large boulder to pebbles.

pokemon.name: Xerneas
pokemon.description: Legends say that when the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is sharing everlasting life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree.

pokemon.name: Xerneas (Active)
pokemon.description: Legends say that when the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is sharing everlasting life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree.

pokemon.name: Yveltal
pokemon.description: When its life comes to an end, its wings and tail spread wide and glow red, and it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon.

pokemon.name: Zygarde (50%-Aura Break)
pokemon.description: It's said to be monitoring the ecosystem from deep in the caves where it lives. There are rumors that even greater power lies hidden within its cells.

pokemon.name: Zygarde (50%-Power Construct)
pokemon.description: It's said to be monitoring the ecosystem from deep in the caves where it lives. There are rumors that even greater power lies hidden within its cells.

pokemon.name: Zygarde (10%-Aura Break)
pokemon.description: This is Zygarde when about 10% of its pieces have been assembled. It leaps at its opponent's chest and sinks its sharp fangs into them.

pokemon.name: Zygarde (10%-Power Construct)
pokemon.description: This is Zygarde when about 10% of its pieces have been assembled. It leaps at its opponent's chest and sinks its sharp fangs into them.

pokemon.name: Zygarde (Complete)
pokemon.description: This is Zygarde's perfected form. From the orifice on its chest, it radiates high-powered energy that eliminates everything.

pokemon.name: Mega-Zygarde
pokemon.description: In response to people's emotions during an unprecedented crisis, Zygarde Mega Evolves and calms the situation with its unmatched power.

pokemon.name: Diancie
pokemon.description: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world. It creates diamonds between its hands.

pokemon.name: Mega-Diancie
pokemon.description: The impurities upon its body's surface have fallen away, sparkling so brilliantly that cannot be observed directly. It is known as “the Royal Pink Princess”.

pokemon.name: Hoopa (Confined)
pokemon.description: In its true form, it possess a huge amount of power. When its powers are sealed away, it is transformed into a much smaller form. It teleports things to a secret place.

pokemon.name: Hoopa (Unbound)
pokemon.description: It is the true form of Hoopa, which has had its power sealed away. The rings it carries have the power to bend dimensions and are able to seize anything in the world.

pokemon.name: Volcanion
pokemon.description: It lets out billows of steam from the arms on its back and disappears into the dense fog. It's said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

pokemon.name: Rowlet
pokemon.description: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night. Silently it glides, drawing near to its target.

pokemon.name: Dartrix
pokemon.description: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.

pokemon.name: Decidueye
pokemon.description: Decidueye is cool and cautious. It fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision, they can pierce a pebble at distances of over a hundred yards.

pokemon.name: Hisuian-Decidueye
pokemon.description: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold.

pokemon.name: Litten
pokemon.description: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

pokemon.name: Torracat
pokemon.description: At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire. With a single punch, it can bend an iron bar right over.

pokemon.name: Incineroar
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has a violent, selfish disposition. If it's not in the mood to listen, it will ignore its Trainer's orders with complete nonchalance.

pokemon.name: Popplio
pokemon.description: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It practices diligently so it can learn to make big bubbles.

pokemon.name: Brionne
pokemon.description: It cares deeply for its companions. When its Trainer is feeling down, it performs a cheery dance with a sequence of water balloons  to try and help.

pokemon.name: Primarina
pokemon.description: It controls its water balloons with song. The melody is learned from others of its kind and is passed down from one generation to the next.

pokemon.name: Pikipek
pokemon.description: This Pokémon feeds on berries, whose leftover seeds become the ammunition for the attacks it fires off from its mouth. It uses holes in trees for nesting.

pokemon.name: Trumbeak
pokemon.description: By bending its beak, it can produce a variety of calls and brand itself a noisy nuisance for its neighbors. It eats berries and stores their seeds in its beak.

pokemon.name: Toucannon
pokemon.description: When it battles, within its beak, its internal gases ignite, explosively launching seeds with enough power to pulverize boulders.

pokemon.name: Yungoos
pokemon.description: With its sharp fangs, it will bite anything. It wanders around in a never-ending search for food. At dusk, it collapses and falls asleep on the spot.

pokemon.name: Gumshoos
pokemon.description: When it finds a trace of its prey, it patiently stakes out the location... but as it's diurnal, it's always snoozing by nightfall.

pokemon.name: Gumshoos (Totem)
pokemon.description: When it finds a trace of its prey, it patiently stakes out the location... but as it's diurnal, it's always snoozing by nightfall.

pokemon.name: Grubbin
pokemon.description: They often gather near places frequented by electric Pokémon in order to avoid being attacked by bird Pokémon, though it normally lives underground.

pokemon.name: Charjabug
pokemon.description: From the food it digests, it generates electricity, and it stores this energy in its electric sac. On camping trips, people are grateful to have one around.

pokemon.name: Vikavolt
pokemon.description: It concentrates electrical energy within its large jaws and uses it to zap its foes. It overwhelms bird Pokémon with shocking beams of electrical energy.

pokemon.name: Vikavolt (Totem)
pokemon.description: It concentrates electrical energy within its large jaws and uses it to zap its foes. It overwhelms bird Pokémon with shocking beams of electrical energy.

pokemon.name: Crabrawler
pokemon.description: While guarding its weak points with its pincers, it looks for an opening and unleashes punches. When it loses, it foams at the mouth and faints.

pokemon.name: Crabominable
pokemon.description: It aimed for the top but got lost and ended up on a snowy mountain. Being forced to endure the cold, this Pokémon evolved and grew thick fur.

pokemon.name: Mega-Crabominable
pokemon.description: It can pulverize reinforced concrete with a light swing of one of its fists, each of which is covered in a thick layer of ice.

pokemon.name: Oricorio (Baile)
pokemon.description: It wins the hearts of its enemies with its passionate dancing and then uses the opening it creates to burn them up with blazing flames.

pokemon.name: Oricorio (Pom Pom)
pokemon.description: This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar. It uses nimble steps to approach opponents, then knocks them out with electric punches.

pokemon.name: Oricorio (Pa'u)
pokemon.description: This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

pokemon.name: Oricorio (Sensu)
pokemon.description: It charms its opponents with its refined dancing. When they let their guard down, it places a curse on them that will bring on their demise.

pokemon.name: Cutiefly
pokemon.description: Myriads of Cutiefly flutter above the heads of people who have auras resembling those of flowers. It can identify which flowers are about to bloom.

pokemon.name: Ribombee
pokemon.description: Ribombee rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle. They are sometimes sold as supplements.

pokemon.name: Ribombee (Totem)
pokemon.description: Ribombee rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle. They are sometimes sold as supplements.

pokemon.name: Rockruff
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has lived with people since times long ago. It can sense when its Trainer is in the dumps and will stick close by its Trainer's side.

pokemon.name: Rockruff (Own Tempo)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has lived with people since times long ago. It can sense when its Trainer is in the dumps and will stick close by its Trainer's side.

pokemon.name: Lycanroc (Midday)
pokemon.description: It has a calm and collected demeanor. It swiftly closes in on its prey, then slices them with the rocks in its mane.

pokemon.name: Lycanroc (Midnight)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.

pokemon.name: Lycanroc (Dusk)
pokemon.description: These Pokémon have both calm and ferocious qualities. It's said that this form of Lycanroc is the most troublesome to raise.

pokemon.name: Wishiwashi (Solo)
pokemon.description: Individually, they're incredibly weak. It's by gathering up into schools that they're able to confront opponents.

pokemon.name: Wishiwashi (School)
pokemon.description: When facing tough opponents, they get into formation. But if they get wounded in battle, they'll scatter and become solitary again.

pokemon.name: Mareanie
pokemon.description: It's found crawling on beaches and seafloors. The coral that grows on Corsola's head is as good as a five-star banquet to this Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Toxapex
pokemon.description: Those attacked by Toxapex's poison will suffer intense pain for three days and three nights. Post-recovery, there will be some aftereffects.

pokemon.name: Mudbray
pokemon.description: The mud stuck to Mudbray's hooves enhances its grip and its powerful running gait. Eating dirt, making mud, and playing in the mire form its daily routine.

pokemon.name: Mudsdale
pokemon.description: Its heavy, mud-covered kicks are its best means of attack, and it can reduce large trucks to scrap without breaking a sweat.

pokemon.name: Dewpider
pokemon.description: It crawls onto the land in search of food. When it comes across enemies or potential prey, this Pokémon smashes its water-bubble-covered head into them.

pokemon.name: Araquanid
pokemon.description: Despite what its appearance suggests, it cares for others. If it finds vulnerable, weak Pokémon, it protectively brings them into its water bubble.

pokemon.name: Araquanid (Totem)
pokemon.description: Despite what its appearance suggests, it cares for others. If it finds vulnerable, weak Pokémon, it protectively brings them into its water bubble.

pokemon.name: Fomantis
pokemon.description: During the day, it sleeps and soaks up light. They give off a sweet and refreshing scent. Cutiefly often gather near the tall grass where Fomantis are hiding.

pokemon.name: Lurantis
pokemon.description: It requires a lot of effort to maintain Lurantis's vivid coloring, but some collectors enjoy this work and treat it as their hobby.

pokemon.name: Lurantis (Totem)
pokemon.description: It requires a lot of effort to maintain Lurantis's vivid coloring, but some collectors enjoy this work and treat it as their hobby.

pokemon.name: Morelull
pokemon.description: As it drowses the day away, it nourishes itself by sucking from tree roots. It wakens at the fall of night, wandering off in search of a new tree.

pokemon.name: Shiinotic
pokemon.description: Forests where Shiinotic live are treacherous to enter at night. People confused by its strange lights can never find their way home again.

pokemon.name: Salandit
pokemon.description: It burns its bodily fluids to create a sweet-smelling poisonous gas. When its enemies become disoriented from inhaling the gas, it attacks them.

pokemon.name: Salazzle
pokemon.description: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit to live with. Its poisonous gas is filled with pheromones.

pokemon.name: Salazzle (Totem)
pokemon.description: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit to live with. Its poisonous gas is filled with pheromones.

pokemon.name: Stufful
pokemon.description: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling. It's an incredibly dangerous Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Bewear
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has the habit of hugging its companions. Many Trainers have left this world after their spines were squashed by its hug.

pokemon.name: Bounsweet
pokemon.description: A delectable aroma pours from its body. Bounsweet's sweat can be watered down into a juice with just the right amount of sweetness.

pokemon.name: Steenee
pokemon.description: The sepals on its head developed to protect its body. These are quite hard, so even if pecked by bird Pokémon, this Pokémon is totally fine.

pokemon.name: Tsareena
pokemon.description: Its long, striking legs aren't just for show but to be used to kick with skill. In victory, it shows off by kicking the defeated, laughing boisterously.

pokemon.name: Comfey
pokemon.description: It attaches flowers to its nutritious vine. Baths prepared with the flowers from its vine have a relaxing effect, so this Pokémon is a hit with many people.

pokemon.name: Oranguru
pokemon.description: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

pokemon.name: Passimian
pokemon.description: They battle with hard berries for weapons. They form groups of about 20 individuals. Their techniques are passed from the boss to the group, generation upon generation.

pokemon.name: Wimpod
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake. It lives on beaches and seabeds.

pokemon.name: Golisopod
pokemon.description: It battles skillfully with its six arms, with a flashing slash of its giant sharp claws, it cleaves seawater--or even air--right in two.

pokemon.name: Mega-Golisopod
pokemon.description: It uses four of its arms to fiercely assail its foes. Once they've been pushed to the brink of defeat, it finishes them off with the arms it kept hidden.

pokemon.name: Sandygast
pokemon.description: It takes control of anyone who puts a hand in its mouth, to add to the pile of its sand-mound body. This Pokémon embodies the grudges of the departed.

pokemon.name: Palossand
pokemon.description: Possessed people controlled by this Pokémon transformed its sand mound into a castle. As it evolved, its power to curse grew ever stronger.

pokemon.name: Pyukumuku
pokemon.description: It lives in shallow seas, such as areas near a beach. The sticky mucous that covers their bodies can be used to soothe sunburned skin. How convenient!

pokemon.name: Type: Null
pokemon.description: Due to the danger that this synthetic Pokémon may go on a rampage, it wears a control mask to restrain its capabilities. It has some hidden special power.

pokemon.name: Silvally (Normal)
pokemon.description: Its trust in its partner awakens it. This Pokémon is capable of changing its type by equipping special memories, a trait that is well displayed in battle.

pokemon.name: Silvally (Fighting)
pokemon.description: Upon awakening, its RKS System is activated. By employing specific memories, this Pokémon can adapt its type to confound its enemies.

pokemon.name: Silvally (Flying)
pokemon.description: Upon awakening, its RKS System is activated. By employing specific memories, this Pokémon can adapt its type to confound its enemies.

pokemon.description: Upon awakening, its RKS System is activated. By employing specific memories, this Pokémon can adapt its type to confound its enemies.

pokemon.name: Minior (Red Meteor)
pokemon.description: Originally making its home in the ozone layer, it hurtles to the ground when the shell encasing its body grows too heavy. It was born from mutated nanoparticles.

pokemon.name: Minior (Red Core)
pokemon.description: If its core stays exposed, it will soon die off. It's possible that it may survive if it's put into a Poké Ball quickly enough.

pokemon.description: Originally making its home in the ozone layer, it hurtles to the ground when the shell encasing its body grows too heavy. It was born from mutated nanoparticles.

pokemon.name: Minior (Orange Core)
