pokemon.name: Komala
pokemon.description: It is born asleep, and it dies asleep. All its movements are apparently no more than the results of it tossing and turning in its dreams.

pokemon.name: Turtonator
pokemon.description: The shell on its back is chemically unstable and explodes violently if struck. The hole in its stomach is its weak point. It gushes fire from its nostrils.

pokemon.name: Togedemaru
pokemon.description: The spiny fur on its back is normally at rest. When this Pokémon becomes agitated, its fur stands on end and stabs into its attackers.

pokemon.name: Togedemaru (Totem)
pokemon.description: The spiny fur on its back is normally at rest. When this Pokémon becomes agitated, its fur stands on end and stabs into its attackers.

pokemon.name: Mimikyu (Disguised)
pokemon.description: A lonely Pokémon, it hides its terrifying appearance beneath an old rag so it can get closer to people and other Pokémon. Its actual appearance is unknown.

pokemon.name: Mimikyu (Busted)
pokemon.description: After going to all the effort of disguising itself, its neck was broken. Whatever is inside is probably unharmed, but it's still feeling sad.

pokemon.name: Mimikyu (Disguised Totem)
pokemon.description: A lonely Pokémon, it hides its terrifying appearance beneath an old rag so it can get closer to people and other Pokémon. Its actual appearance is unknown.

pokemon.name: Mimikyu (Busted Totem)
pokemon.description: After going to all the effort of disguising itself, its neck was broken. Whatever is inside is probably unharmed, but it's still feeling sad.

pokemon.name: Bruxish
pokemon.description: It stuns its prey with its psychic powers and then grinds them to mush with its strong teeth. Even Shellder's shell is no match for it.

pokemon.name: Drampa
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is friendly to people and loves children most of all. It comes from deep in the mountains to play with children it likes in town.

pokemon.name: Mega-Drampa
pokemon.description: Drampa's cells have been invigorated, allowing it to regain its youth. It manipulates the atmosphere to summon storms.

pokemon.name: Dhelmise
pokemon.description: The soul of seaweed adrift in the waves became reborn as this Pokémon. It maintains itself with new infusions of seabed detritus and seaweed.

pokemon.name: Jangmo-o
pokemon.description: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o live. They grow little by little battling one another.

pokemon.name: Hakamo-o
pokemon.description: It sheds and regrows its scales on a continuous basis. The scales become harder each time they're regrown. Its scaly punches tear its foes to shreds.

pokemon.name: Kommo-o
pokemon.description: Its rigid scales function as offense and defense. In the past, its scales were processed and used to make weapons and other valuable commodities.

pokemon.name: Kommo-o (Totem)
pokemon.description: Its rigid scales function as offense and defense. In the past, its scales were processed and used to make weapons and other valuable commodities.

pokemon.name: Tapu Koko
pokemon.description: It confuses its enemies by flying too quickly for the eye to follow. It has a hair-trigger temper but forgets what made it angry an instant later.

pokemon.name: Tapu Lele
pokemon.description: A fragrant aroma of flowers follows it. As it flutters about, it scatters its strangely glowing scales. Touching them is said to restore good health.

pokemon.name: Tapu Bulu
pokemon.description: It causes vegetation to grow, and then it absorbs energy from the growth. It pulls large trees up by the roots and swings them around at its enemies.

pokemon.name: Tapu Fini
pokemon.description: People say it can create pure water that will wash away any corruption. The dense fog it creates brings the downfall and destruction of its confused enemies.

pokemon.name: Cosmog
pokemon.description: In ages past, it was called the child of the stars. It's said to be a Pokémon from another world, but no specific details are known.

pokemon.name: Cosmoem
pokemon.description: Motionless as if dead, its body is faintly warm to the touch. There's something accumulating around the black core within its hard shell.

pokemon.name: Solgaleo
pokemon.description: It is said to live in another world. The intense light it radiates from the surface of its body can make the darkest of nights light up like midday.

pokemon.name: Lunala
pokemon.description: When its third eye activates, away it flies to another world. This Pokémon devours light, drawing the moonless dark veil of night over the brightness of day.

pokemon.name: Nihilego
pokemon.description: One of several mysterious Ultra Beasts. It's unclear whether or not this Pokémon is sentient, but sometimes it can be observed behaving like a young girl.

pokemon.name: Buzzwole
pokemon.description: This life-form called an Ultra Beast appeared from another world. It shows off its body, but whether that display is a boast or a threat remains unclear.

pokemon.name: Pheromosa
pokemon.description: One of the dangerous Ultra Beasts, it refuses to touch anything, perhaps because it senses some uncleanness in this world.

pokemon.name: Xurkitree
pokemon.description: One of the mysterious life-forms known as Ultra Beasts. Astonishing electric shocks emanate from its entire body, according to witnesses.

pokemon.name: Celesteela
pokemon.description: It appeared from the Ultra Wormhole. One kind of Ultra Beast, witnesses saw it flying across the sky by expelling gas from its two arms.

pokemon.name: Kartana
pokemon.description: This Ultra Beast came from the Ultra Wormhole. It seems not to attack enemies on its own, but its sharp body is a dangerous weapon in itself.

pokemon.name: Guzzlord
pokemon.description: A dangerous Ultra Beast, it has gobbled mountains and swallowed whole buildings, according to reports. But for some reason its droppings have never been found.

pokemon.name: Necrozma
pokemon.description: Reminiscent of the Ultra Beasts, this life-form, apparently asleep underground, is thought to have come from another world in ancient times.

pokemon.name: Necrozma (Dusk Mane)
pokemon.description: This is its form while it is devouring the light of Solgaleo. It pounces on foes and then slashes them with the claws on its four limbs and back.

pokemon.name: Necrozma (Dawn Wings)
pokemon.description: This is its form while it's devouring the light of Lunala. It grasps foes in its giant claws and rips them apart with brute force.

pokemon.name: Necrozma (Ultra)
pokemon.description: The light pouring out from all over its body affects living things and nature, impacting them in various ways.

pokemon.name: Magearna
pokemon.description: This artificial Pokémon, constructed more than 500 years ago, can understand human speech but cannot itself speak. Its true self is its Soul-Heart, an artificial soul.

pokemon.name: Magearna (Original)
pokemon.description: This is its form from almost 500 years ago. Its body is nothing more than a container-its artificial heart is the actual life-form.

pokemon.name: Mega-Magearna
pokemon.description: This artificial Pokémon, constructed more than 500 years ago, can understand human speech but cannot itself speak. Its true self is its Soul-Heart, an artificial soul.

pokemon.name: Mega-Magearna (Original)
pokemon.description: A mechanism to remove Magearna's limitations has lain secretly within Magearna for 500 years. This mechanism is triggered by a Mega Stone.

pokemon.name: Marshadow
pokemon.description: Able to conceal itself in the shadows of others, it never appears before humans, so its very existence is the stuff of myth. This Pokémon is craven and cowering.

pokemon.name: Poipole
pokemon.description: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

pokemon.name: Naganadel
pokemon.description: It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body. It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

pokemon.name: Stakataka
pokemon.description: It appeared from an Ultra Wormhole. Each one appears to be made up of many life- forms stacked one on top of each other.

pokemon.name: Blacephalon
pokemon.description: It slithers toward people and explode its head without warning. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.

pokemon.name: Zeraora
pokemon.description: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

pokemon.name: Mega-Zeraora
pokemon.description: It stores up 10 lightning strikes' worth of electricity. When it stops limiting itself, it's in the strongest class of electric Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Meltan
pokemon.description: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

pokemon.name: Melmetal
pokemon.description: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

pokemon.name: GMax-Melmetal
pokemon.description: In a distant land, there are legends about a cyclopean giant. In fact, the giant was a Melmetal that was flooded with Gigantamax energy.

pokemon.name: Grookey
pokemon.description: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

pokemon.name: Thwackey
pokemon.description: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

pokemon.name: Rillaboom
pokemon.description: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

pokemon.name: GMax-Rillaboom
pokemon.description: Rillaboom has become one with its forest of drums and continues to lay down beats that shake all of Galar.

pokemon.name: Scorbunny
pokemon.description: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.

pokemon.name: Raboot
pokemon.description: Its thick and fluffy fur protects it from the cold and enables it to use hotter fire moves.

pokemon.name: Cinderace
pokemon.description: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.

pokemon.name: GMax-Cinderace
pokemon.description: Infused with Cinderace's fighting spirit, the gigantic Pyro Ball never misses its targets and completely roasts opponents.

pokemon.name: Sobble
pokemon.description: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

pokemon.name: Drizzile
pokemon.description: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

pokemon.name: Inteleon
pokemon.description: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

pokemon.name: GMax-Inteleon
pokemon.description: Gigantamax Inteleon's Water Gun move fires at Mach 7. As the Pokémon takes aim, it uses the crest on its head to gauge wind and temperature.

pokemon.name: Skwovet
pokemon.description: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

pokemon.name: Greedent
pokemon.description: It stashes berries in its tail--so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn't notice the loss.

pokemon.name: Rookidee
pokemon.description: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle--even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

pokemon.name: Corvisquire
pokemon.description: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.

pokemon.name: Corviknight
pokemon.description: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

pokemon.name: GMax-Corviknight
pokemon.description: Imbued with Gigantamax energy, its wings can whip up winds more forceful than any a hurricane could muster. The gusts blow everything away.

pokemon.name: Blipbug
pokemon.description: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn't.

pokemon.name: Dottler
pokemon.description: It barely moves, but it's still alive. Hiding in its shell without food or water seems to have awakened its psychic powers.

pokemon.name: Orbeetle
pokemon.description: It's famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.

pokemon.name: GMax-Orbeetle
pokemon.description: Its brain has grown to a gargantuan size, as has the rest of its body. This Pokémon's intellect and psychic abilities are overpowering.

pokemon.name: Nickit
pokemon.description: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

pokemon.name: Thievul
pokemon.description: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

pokemon.name: Gossifleur
pokemon.description: It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.

pokemon.name: Eldegoss
pokemon.description: The seeds attached to its cotton fluff are full of nutrients. It spreads them on the wind so that plants and other Pokémon can benefit from them.

pokemon.name: Wooloo
pokemon.description: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

pokemon.name: Dubwool
pokemon.description: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You'll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

pokemon.name: Chewtle
pokemon.description: Apparently the itch of its teething impels it to snap its jaws at anything in front of it.

pokemon.name: Drednaw
pokemon.description: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.

pokemon.name: GMax-Drednaw
pokemon.description: In the Galar region, there's a tale about this Pokémon chewing up a mountain and using the rubble to stop a flood.

pokemon.name: Yamper
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

pokemon.name: Boltund
pokemon.description: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

pokemon.name: Rolycoly
pokemon.description: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

pokemon.name: Carkol
pokemon.description: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.

pokemon.name: Coalossal
pokemon.description: It's usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

pokemon.name: GMax-Coalossal
pokemon.description: Its body is a colossal stove. With Gigantamax energy stoking the fire, this Pokémon's flame burns hotter than 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

pokemon.name: Applin
pokemon.description: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it's just an apple and nothing more.

pokemon.name: Flapple
pokemon.description: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

pokemon.name: GMax-Flapple
pokemon.description: Under the influence of Gigantamax energy, it produces much more sweet nectar, and its shape has changed to resemble a giant apple.

pokemon.name: Appletun
pokemon.description: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

pokemon.name: GMax-Appletun
pokemon.description: Due to Gigantamax energy, this Pokémon's nectar has thickened. The increased viscosity lets the nectar absorb more damage than before.

pokemon.name: Dipplin
pokemon.description: Dipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.

pokemon.name: Hydrapple
pokemon.description: These capricious syrpents have banded together. On the rare occasion that their moods align, their true power is unleashed.

pokemon.name: Silicobra
pokemon.description: As it digs, it swallows sand and stores it in its neck pouch. The pouch can hold more than 17 pounds of sand.

pokemon.name: Sandaconda
pokemon.description: When it contracts its body, over 220 pounds of sand sprays from its nose. If it ever runs out of sand, it becomes disheartened.

pokemon.name: GMax-Sandaconda
pokemon.description: Its sand pouch has grown to tremendous proportions. More than 1,000,000 tons of sand now swirl around its body with enough speed and power to pulverize a skyscraper.

pokemon.name: Cramorant
pokemon.description: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

pokemon.name: Cramorant (Gulping)
pokemon.description: Cramorant's gluttony led it to try to swallow an Arrokuda whole, which in turn led to Cramorant getting an Arrokuda stuck in its throat.

pokemon.name: Cramorant (Gorging)
pokemon.description: This Cramorant has accidentally gotten a Pikachu lodged in its gullet. Cramorant is choking a little, but it isn't really bothered.

pokemon.name: Arrokuda
pokemon.description: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It's very proud of that jaw.

pokemon.name: Barraskewda
pokemon.description: This Pokémon has a jaw that's as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda's flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

pokemon.name: Toxel
pokemon.description: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

pokemon.name: Toxtricity (Amped)
pokemon.description: When this Pokémon sounds as if it's strumming a guitar, it's actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

pokemon.name: GMax-Toxtricity (Amped)
pokemon.description: Out of control after its own poison penetrated its brain, it tears across the land in a rampage, contaminating the earth with toxic sweat.

pokemon.name: Toxtricity (Low Key)
pokemon.description: Capable of generating 15,000 volts of electricity, this Pokémon looks down on all that would challenge it.

pokemon.name: GMax-Toxtricity (Low Key)
pokemon.description: Out of control after its own poison penetrated its brain, it tears across the land in a rampage, contaminating the earth with toxic sweat.

pokemon.name: Sizzlipede
pokemon.description: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

pokemon.name: Centiskorch
pokemon.description: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

pokemon.name: GMax-Centiskorch
pokemon.description: The heat that comes off a Gigantamax Centiskorch may destabilize air currents. Sometimes it can even cause storms.

pokemon.name: Clobbopus
pokemon.description: It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

pokemon.name: Grapploct
pokemon.description: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

pokemon.name: Sinistea (Phony)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

pokemon.name: Sinistea (Antique)
pokemon.description: The swirl pattern in this Pokémon's body is its weakness. If it gets stirred, the swirl loses its shape, and Sinistea gets dizzy.

pokemon.name: Polteageist (Phony)
pokemon.description: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

pokemon.name: Polteageist (Antique)
pokemon.description: Trainers Polteageist trusts will be allowed to experience its distinctive flavor and aroma firsthand by sampling just a tiny bit of its tea.

pokemon.name: Hatenna
pokemon.description: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures' emotions. If you don't have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

pokemon.name: Hattrem
pokemon.description: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

pokemon.name: Hatterene
pokemon.description: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

pokemon.name: GMax-Hatterene
pokemon.description: This Pokémon can read the emotions of creatures over 30 miles away. The minute it senses hostility, it goes on the attack.

pokemon.name: Impidimp
pokemon.description: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

pokemon.name: Morgrem
pokemon.description: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it's trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

pokemon.name: Grimmsnarl
pokemon.description: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

pokemon.name: GMax-Grimmsnarl
pokemon.description: Gigantamax energy has caused more hair to sprout all over its body. With the added strength, it can jump over the world's tallest building.

pokemon.name: Milcery
pokemon.description: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

pokemon.name: Alcremie (Vanilla Cream Strawberry)
pokemon.description: If Alcremie is content, the secreted cream from its hands becomes sweeter and richer. When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it's decorated with cream.

pokemon.name: GMax-Alcremie
pokemon.description: It launches swarms of missiles, each made of cream and loaded with 100,000 kilocalories. Get hit by one of these, and your head will swim.

pokemon.name: Falinks
pokemon.description: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass's orders are absolute.

pokemon.name: Mega-Falinks
pokemon.description: Mega Falinks has taken on the ultimate battle formation, which can be achieved only if the troopers and brass have the strongest of bonds.

pokemon.name: Pincurchin
pokemon.description: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

pokemon.name: Snom
pokemon.description: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

pokemon.name: Frosmoth
pokemon.description: Icy scales fall from its wings like snow as it flies over fields and mountains. The temperature of its wings is less than -290 degrees Fahrenheit.

pokemon.name: Stonjourner
pokemon.description: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

pokemon.name: Eiscue (Ice)
pokemon.description: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

pokemon.name: Eiscue (Noice)
pokemon.description: The hair on its head connects to the surface of its brain. When this Pokémon has something on its mind, its hair chills the air around it.

pokemon.name: Indeedee (♂)
pokemon.description: It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.

pokemon.name: Indeedee (♀)
pokemon.description: They diligently serve people and Pokémon so they can gather feelings of gratitude. The females are particularly good at babysitting.

pokemon.name: Morpeko (Full Belly)
pokemon.description: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

pokemon.name: Morpeko (Hangry)
pokemon.description: Intense hunger drives it to extremes of violence, and the electricity in its cheek sacs has converted into a Dark-type energy.

pokemon.name: Cufant
pokemon.description: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It's also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

pokemon.name: Copperajah
pokemon.description: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.

pokemon.name: GMax-Copperajah
pokemon.description: After this Pokémon has Gigantamaxed, its massive nose can utterly demolish large structures with a single smashing blow.

pokemon.name: Dracozolt
pokemon.description: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

pokemon.name: Arctozolt
pokemon.description: The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

pokemon.name: Dracovish
pokemon.description: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

pokemon.name: Arctovish
pokemon.description: Though it's able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

pokemon.name: Duraludon
pokemon.description: Its body resembles polished metal, and it's both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

pokemon.name: GMax-Duraludon
pokemon.description: The hardness of its cells is exceptional, even among Steel types. It also has a body structure that's resistant to earthquakes.

pokemon.name: Archaludon
pokemon.description: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

pokemon.name: Dreepy
pokemon.description: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

pokemon.name: Drakloak
pokemon.description: It's capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

pokemon.name: Dragapult
pokemon.description: When it isn't battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

pokemon.name: Zacian (Hero)
pokemon.description: Known as a legendary hero, this Pokémon absorbs metal particles, transforming them into a weapon it uses to battle.

pokemon.name: Zacian (Crowned)
pokemon.description: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King's Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

pokemon.name: Zamazenta (Hero)
pokemon.description: In times past, it worked together with a king of the people to save the Galar region. It absorbs metal that it then uses in battle.

pokemon.name: Zamazenta (Crowned)
pokemon.description: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

pokemon.name: Eternatus
pokemon.description: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

pokemon.name: Eternamax-Eternatus
pokemon.description: As a result of Rose's meddling, Eternatus absorbed all the energy in the Galar region. It's now in a state of power overload.

pokemon.name: Kubfu
pokemon.description: Kubfu trains hard to perfect its moves. The moves it masters will determine which form it takes when it evolves.

pokemon.name: Urshifu (Single Strike)
pokemon.description: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in the one-hit KO. Its strategy is to leap in close to foes and land a devastating blow with a hardened fist.

pokemon.name: GMax-Urshifu (Singe Strike)
pokemon.description: People call it the embodiment of rage. It's said that this Pokémon's terrifying expression and shout will rid the world of malevolence.

pokemon.name: Urshifu (Rapid Strike)
pokemon.description: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in defeating foes by raining many blows down on them. Its strikes are nonstop, flowing like a river.

pokemon.name: GMax-Urshifu (Rapid Strike)
pokemon.description: As it waits for the right moment to unleash its Gigantamax power, this Pokémon maintains a perfect one- legged stance. It won't even twitch.

pokemon.name: Zarude
pokemon.description: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It's incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

pokemon.name: Zarude (Dada)
pokemon.description: This Zarude's special strength stems from its love and care for an orphaned human child that the Pokémon has raised.

pokemon.name: Regieleki
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is a cluster of electrical energy. It's said that removing the rings on Regieleki's body will unleash the Pokémon's latent power.

pokemon.name: Regidrago
pokemon.description: An academic theory proposes that Regidrago's arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.

pokemon.name: Glastrier
pokemon.description: Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It's also a belligerent Pokémon-- anything it wants, it takes by force.

pokemon.name: Spectrier
pokemon.description: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one--it doesn't use its sense of sight. Spectrier's kicks are said to separate soul from body.

pokemon.name: Calyrex
pokemon.description: Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.

pokemon.name: Calyrex (Ice Rider)
pokemon.description: According to lore, this Pokémon showed no mercy to those who got in its way, yet it would heal its opponents' wounds after battle.

pokemon.name: Calyrex (Shadow Rider)
pokemon.description: It's said that Calyrex and a Pokémon that had bonded with it ran all across the Galar region to bring green to the wastelands.

pokemon.name: Enamorus (Incarnate)
pokemon.description: Its arrival brings an end to the winter. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across the land.

pokemon.name: Enamorus (Therian)
pokemon.description: From the clouds, it descends upon those who treat any form of life with disrespect and metes out wrathful, ruthless punishment.

pokemon.name: Sprigatito
pokemon.description: The sweet scent its body gives off mesmerizes those around it. The scent grows stronger when this Pokémon is in the sun.

pokemon.name: Floragato
pokemon.description: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

pokemon.name: Meowscarada
pokemon.description: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

pokemon.name: Fuecoco
pokemon.description: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco's head and flickers to and fro.

pokemon.name: Crocalor
pokemon.description: The combination of Crocalor's fire energy and overflowing vitality has caused an egg-shaped fireball to appear on the Pokémon's head.

pokemon.name: Skeledirge
pokemon.description: Skeledirge's gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.

pokemon.name: Quaxly
pokemon.description: Its strong legs let it easily swim around in even fast-flowing rivers. It likes to keep things tidy and is prone to overthinking things.

pokemon.name: Quaxwell
pokemon.description: The hardworking Quaxwell observes people and Pokémon from various regions and incorporates their movements into its own dance routines.

pokemon.name: Quaquaval
pokemon.description: A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.

pokemon.name: Lechonk
pokemon.description: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

pokemon.name: Oinkologne (♂)
pokemon.description: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

pokemon.name: Oinkologne (♀)
pokemon.description: This is a meticulous Pokémon that likes to keep things tidy. It shrouds itself in a floral aroma that soothes the Pokémon around it.

pokemon.name: Tarountula
pokemon.description: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

pokemon.name: Spidops
pokemon.description: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

pokemon.name: Nymble
pokemon.description: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

pokemon.name: Lokix
pokemon.description: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

pokemon.name: Pawmi
pokemon.description: The pads of its paws are electricity- discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

pokemon.name: Pawmo
pokemon.description: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

pokemon.name: Pawmot
pokemon.description: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

pokemon.name: Tandemaus
pokemon.description: Exhibiting great teamwork, they use their incisors to cut pieces out of any material that might be useful for a nest, then make off with them.

pokemon.name: Maushold (Three)
pokemon.description: The little one just appeared one day. They all live together like a family, but the relationship between the three is still unclear.

pokemon.name: Maushold (Four)
pokemon.description: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

pokemon.name: Fidough
pokemon.description: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

pokemon.name: Dachsbun
pokemon.description: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon's body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

pokemon.name: Smoliv
pokemon.description: This Pokémon converts nutrients into oil, which it stores in the fruit on its head. It can easily go a whole week without eating or drinking.

pokemon.name: Dolliv
pokemon.description: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh- scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

pokemon.name: Arboliva
pokemon.description: This Pokémon drives back enemies by launching its rich, aromatic oil at them with enough force to smash a boulder.

pokemon.name: Squawkabilly (Green)
pokemon.description: Green-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they're out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.

pokemon.name: Squawkabilly (Blue)
pokemon.description: The largest of their flocks can contain more than 50 individuals. They fly around towns and forests, searching for food and making a racket.

pokemon.name: Squawkabilly (Yellow)
pokemon.description: These Squawkabilly are hotheaded, and their fighting style is vicious. They'll leap within reach of their foes to engage in close combat.

pokemon.name: Squawkabilly (White)
pokemon.description: Though these Squawkabilly are the fewest in number, they have no trouble living in towns since they blend in with the white buildings.

pokemon.name: Nacli
pokemon.description: The ground scrapes its body as it travels, causing it to leave salt behind. Salt is constantly being created and replenished inside Nacli's body.

pokemon.name: Naclstack
pokemon.description: It compresses rock salt inside its body and shoots out hardened salt pellets with enough force to perforate an iron sheet.

pokemon.name: Garganacl
pokemon.description: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

pokemon.name: Charcadet
pokemon.description: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

pokemon.name: Armarouge
pokemon.description: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

pokemon.name: Ceruledge
pokemon.description: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

pokemon.name: Tadbulb
pokemon.description: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

pokemon.name: Bellibolt
pokemon.description: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly- button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

pokemon.name: Wattrel
pokemon.description: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

pokemon.name: Kilowattrel
pokemon.description: It uses its throat sac to store electricity generated by its wings. There's hardly any oil in its feathers, so it is a poor swimmer.

pokemon.name: Maschiff
pokemon.description: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

pokemon.name: Mabosstiff
pokemon.description: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

pokemon.name: Shroodle
pokemon.description: To keep enemies away from its territory, it paints markings around its nest using a poisonous liquid that has an acrid odor.

pokemon.name: Grafaiai
pokemon.description: Each Grafaiai paints its own individual pattern, and it will paint that same pattern over and over again throughout its life.

pokemon.name: Bramblin
pokemon.description: A soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Brambleghast
pokemon.description: It will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.

pokemon.name: Toedscool
pokemon.description: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

pokemon.name: Toedscruel
pokemon.description: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

pokemon.name: Klawf
pokemon.description: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

pokemon.name: Capsakid
pokemon.description: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.

pokemon.name: Scovillain
pokemon.description: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

pokemon.name: Mega-Scovillain
pokemon.description: Mega Evolution has dialed up this Pokémon's spiciness. It swings its “necktie” around to wallop its foes.

pokemon.name: Rellor
pokemon.description: This Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.

pokemon.name: Rabsca
pokemon.description: The body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.

pokemon.name: Flittle
pokemon.description: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

pokemon.name: Espathra
pokemon.description: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

pokemon.name: Tinkatink
pokemon.description: This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it's satisfied with the result.

pokemon.name: Tinkatuff
pokemon.description: These Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other's hammers by smashing them together.

pokemon.name: Tinkaton
pokemon.description: The hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.

pokemon.name: Wiglett
pokemon.description: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

pokemon.name: Wugtrio
pokemon.description: A variety of fish Pokémon, Wugtrio was once considered to be a regional form of Dugtrio.

pokemon.name: Bombirdier
pokemon.description: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

pokemon.name: Finizen
pokemon.description: Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

pokemon.name: Palafin (Zero)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon changes its appearance if it hears its allies calling for help. Palafin will never show anybody its moment of transformation.

pokemon.name: Palafin (Hero)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon's ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

pokemon.name: Varoom
pokemon.description: The steel section is Varoom's actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

pokemon.name: Revavroom
pokemon.description: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders- now numbering eight-to generate energy.

pokemon.name: Cyclizar
pokemon.description: It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider's body heat warms Cyclizar's back and lifts the Pokémon's spirit.

pokemon.name: Orthworm
pokemon.description: When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.

pokemon.name: Glimmet
pokemon.description: Glimmet's toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.

pokemon.name: Glimmora
pokemon.description: Glimmora's petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.

pokemon.name: Mega-Glimmora
pokemon.description: Glimmora's petals-now larger and separated from its main body-rotate around it to provide defense while cattering poisonous fragments.

pokemon.name: Greavard
pokemon.description: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

pokemon.name: Houndstone
pokemon.description: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

pokemon.name: Flamigo
pokemon.description: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

pokemon.name: Cetoddle
pokemon.description: This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.

pokemon.name: Cetitan
pokemon.description: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

pokemon.name: Veluza
pokemon.description: Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.

pokemon.name: Dondozo
pokemon.description: It treats Tatsugiri like its boss and follows it loyally. Though powerful, Dondozo is apparently not very smart.

pokemon.name: Tatsugiri (Curly)
pokemon.description: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

pokemon.name: Mega-Tatsugiri (Curly)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon tricks its opponents by playing dead. It is small and weak, but it uses its smarts to survive.

pokemon.name: Tatsugiri (Droopy)
pokemon.description: It's one of the most intelligent dragon Pokémon. It camouflages itself by inflating its throat sac.

pokemon.name: Mega-Tatsugiri (Droopy)
pokemon.description: Tatsugiri's brain has been invigorated by Mega Evolution, making it even wilier. It can create and command copies of itself.

pokemon.name: Tatsugiri (Stretchy)
pokemon.description: It solidifies the energy of Mega Evolution, building up an overflowing pile to launch as projectiles. These projectiles explode on contact.

pokemon.name: Mega-Tatsugiri (Stretchy)
pokemon.description: Using the energy of Mega Evolution, it creates a dish to ride upon, allowing it to move with total freedom-even through the air.

pokemon.name: Great Tusk
pokemon.description: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

pokemon.name: Scream Tail
pokemon.description: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

pokemon.name: Brute Bonnet
pokemon.description: It bears a slight resemblance to a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a cross between a dinosaur and a mushroom.

pokemon.name: Flutter Mane
pokemon.description: It has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.

pokemon.name: Slither Wing
pokemon.description: This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.

pokemon.name: Sandy Shocks
pokemon.description: It slightly resembles a Magneton that lived for 10,000 years and was featured in an article in a paranormal magazine.

pokemon.name: Iron Treads
pokemon.description: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

pokemon.name: Iron Bundle
pokemon.description: Its shape is similar to a robot featured in a paranormal magazine article. The robot was said to have been created by an ancient civilization.

pokemon.name: Iron Hands
pokemon.description: It is very similar to a cyborg covered exclusively by a paranormal magazine. The cyborg was said to be the modified form of a certain athlete.

pokemon.name: Iron Jugulis
pokemon.description: It resembles a certain Pokémon introduced in a paranormal magazine, described as the offspring of a Hydreigon that fell in love with a robot.

pokemon.name: Iron Moth
pokemon.description: No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon's traits match up with an object described in an old book.

pokemon.name: Iron Thorns
pokemon.description: It has some similarities to a Pokémon introduced in a dubious magazine as a Tyranitar from one billion years into the future.

pokemon.name: Frigibax
pokemon.description: This Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.

pokemon.name: Arctibax
pokemon.description: Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.

pokemon.name: Baxcalibur
pokemon.description: It launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.

pokemon.name: Mega-Baxcalibur
pokemon.description: Baxcalibur's dorsal blade has grown even more massive thanks to Mega Evolution. This Pokémon fires beams from the hilt at its solar plexus.

pokemon.name: Gimmighoul (Chest)
pokemon.description: It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it's actually a Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Gimmighoul (Roaming)
pokemon.description: It wanders around, carrying an old coin on its back. It survives by draining the life-force from humans who try to pick up its coin.

pokemon.name: Gholdengo
pokemon.description: It has a sturdy body made up of stacked coins. Gholdengo overwhelms its enemies by firing coin after coin at them in quick succession.

pokemon.name: Wo-Chien
pokemon.description: The grudge of a person punished for writing the king's evil deeds upon wooden tablets has clad itself in dead leaves to become a Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Chien-Pao
pokemon.description: This Pokémon can control 100 tons of fallen snow. It plays around innocently by leaping in and out of avalanches it has caused.

pokemon.name: Ting-Lu
pokemon.description: It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

pokemon.name: Chi-Yu
pokemon.description: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

pokemon.name: Roaring Moon
pokemon.description: According to an article in a dubious magazine, this Pokémon has some connection to Mega Evolution that occurs in Hoenn.

pokemon.name: Iron Valiant
pokemon.description: It has some similarities to a mad scientist's invention covered in a paranormal magazine.

pokemon.name: Koraidon
pokemon.description: This Pokémon resembles Cyclizar, but it is far burlier and more ferocious. Nothing is known about its ecology or other features.

pokemon.name: Miraidon
pokemon.description: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an Violet Book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

pokemon.name: Walking Wake
pokemon.description: This ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It's named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old journal.

pokemon.name: Iron Leaves
pokemon.description: According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

pokemon.name: Poltchageist (Counterfeit)
pokemon.description: Supposedly, the regrets of a tea ceremony master who died before perfecting his craft lingered in some matcha and became a Pokémon.

pokemon.name: Poltchageist (Artisan)
pokemon.description: It has taken up residence in a very expensive tea caddy. It takes an expert to distinguish the expensive tea caddies from the cheap ones.

pokemon.name: Sinistcha (Unremarkable)
pokemon.description: It pretends to be tea, trying to fool people into drinking it so it can drain their life-force. Its ruse is generally unsuccessful.

pokemon.name: Sinistcha (Masterpiece)
pokemon.description: The more stirring it does with the tea whisk on its head, the more energy it builds up. It does this to prepare for battle.

pokemon.name: Okidogi
pokemon.description: After all its muscles were stimulated by the toxic chain around its neck, Okidogi transformed and gained a powerful physique.

pokemon.name: Munkidori
pokemon.description: The chain is made from toxins that enhance capabilities. It stimulated Munkidori's brain and caused the Pokémon's psychic powers to bloom.

pokemon.name: Fezandipiti
pokemon.description: Fezandipiti owes its beautiful looks and lovely voice to the toxic stimulants emanating from the chain wrapped around its body.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Teal Mask)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Wellspring Mask)
pokemon.description: This form excels in both attack and defense. It ceaselessly unleashes moves like a spring gushes water.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Hearthflame Mask)
pokemon.description: This form is the most aggressive, bombarding enemies with the intensity of flames blazing within a hearth.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Cornerstone Mask)
pokemon.description: In this form, it draws on the power of stone. Its body is rock-solid, protecting it from all manner of attacks.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Teal Mask Tera)
pokemon.description: This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Wellspring Mask Tera)
pokemon.description: This form excels in both attack and defense. It ceaselessly unleashes moves like a spring gushes water.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Hearthflame Mask Tera)
pokemon.description: This form is the most aggressive, bombarding enemies with the intensity of flames blazing within a hearth.

pokemon.name: Ogerpon (Cornerstone Mask Tera)
pokemon.description: In this form, it draws on the power of stone. Its body is rock-solid, protecting it from all manner of attacks.

pokemon.name: Gouging Fire
pokemon.description: There are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging, and spouting pillars of flame.

pokemon.name: Raging Bolt
pokemon.description: It bears resemblance to a Pokémon that became a hot topic for a short while after a paranomal magazine touted it as Raikou's ancestor.

pokemon.name: Iron Boulder
pokemon.description: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

pokemon.name: Iron Crown
pokemon.description: It resembles a mysterious object introduced in a paranormal magazine as a cutting-edge weapon shaped like Cobalion.

pokemon.name: Terapagos (Normal)
pokemon.description: Terapagos protects itself using its power to transform energy into hard crystals. This Pokémon is the source of the Terastal phenomenon.

pokemon.name: Terapagos (Terastal)
pokemon.description: The shell is made of crystallized Terastal energy. When struck by a move, this shell absorbs the move's energy and transfers it to Terapagos.

pokemon.name: Terapagos (Stellar)
pokemon.description: An old expedition journal describes the sight of this Pokémon buried in the, depths of the earth as resembling a planet floating in space.

pokemon.name: Pecharunt
pokemon.description: It feeds others toxic mochi that draw out desires and capabilities. Those who eat the mochi fall under Pecharunt's control, chained to its will.
